Title: Chapter : 09
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

Ishan looks at me. He wants me to say something in his support. I open my mouth to speak, wanting to support him, but all I do is open and close it, unable to find the words. For the first time since Ishan arrived here, I see disappointment in his eyes.FOR ME.I should have spoken up for him, but I couldn't come up with anything.

Whatever they are suggesting seems like the only option that is left for us. Ishan is actively opposing the idea of fake dating, and my phone starts buzzing. It's Shreyas. "Oh, shit," I mutter under my breath and tap the green icon.

"Sorry, Shreyas, something urgent came up, so I won't be able to go with you guys," I say without letting him say hello. I totally forgot about our trip plan because of all this chaos.

"Haan pr ho kya gya?"

"I will explain everything but not now, okay bye"

"But-" I disconnect the call before he could ask anything, seriously I am not in a position to describe this scenario to anyone right now.

After 10 minutes, Ishan stops protesting against the idea and sit there with a defeated look. And now he is definitely pissed, pissed atME.

"Okay so from now on Shubman Gill and Ishan Kishan are in a relationship or let's say in a fake relationship which means In public eyes they are couples and you two should behave like that only"

"Huh?" Ishan ask raising his eyebrows.

"I am not asking for you two to kiss in public, just don't let your gaurd's down and keep in mind that you two are in a relationship especially when you are in any public place." Nia says while closing her laptop and getting up.

"Okay now I have to go somewhere." she collected her things and about to leave the room when darshan speaks "I'll drop you." I narrow my eyes at him. he never offered me this service.

Ishan stand up, I notice his clothes he is wearing a dark purple t-shirt and a cargo pant. His t-shirt says :

"THAT'S WHAT IT WAS. IT WAS WHAT THE F*CK."

Bruh, he literally chose to wear this t shirt here, he definitely has a collection of t shirts with some weird funny lines printer on them, last time it was- ah what it was? Nevermind.

---

It's been 3 day since Ishan and I came into a relationship well fake relationship. I was able to get his number, and with lot of courage I messaged him, but that was in vain:

Me:Hello IshanIt's me Shubman

Ishan:We will talk later, Shubman.

It's been three days and he didn't bother to talk, first thing first I need to apologise to him, and also make him understand that whatever we are doing is for our own good and especially for ICT's image.

One good thing happened though, Virat bhaiya bought a farm house and he is throwing a party there, which means I'll get to meet Ishan there and have a conversation with him.

---

Ishan is sitting with Shreyas and Hardik bhai, talking to them without acknowledging my presence. The party is on full swing. I am with yuzi Bhai as he is telling me about the prank he pulled on team members.

After half an hour, when I look towards Ishan, Shreyas waves towards me, gesturing for me to go there. At first, I thought he wants me to join them, but that's not the case here.

When I reach where they are sitting, I notice that Ishan's head is resting on the table. "Shubman, can you bring him to his room?" Shreyas asks me. I look at Ishan and then again at Shreyas. He senses my confusion and continues, "Actually, Ishan accidentally had an alcoholic drink, thinking that it was non-alcoholic."

"And I guess he's never had any alcoholic drink before, as he seems pretty high after just one drink," Hardik Bhai chimes in.

Both Shreyas and Hardik Bhai look a little bit drunk, but they are still in their senses and can talk properly.

"Uhh okay" I say with a little bit ofskepticism.

When my hand reaches his arm, he looks up, his nose and cheeks are a dark shade of pink, and his eyes are droopy. He actually looks pretty high.

"Let's go to your room," I say, putting his arm over my shoulders, with one hand on his waist and the other holding his hand. He is able to walk with my support. We reach the part where our rooms are,his room is next to mine. As I am about to open the door, he speaks up, "My... brace.........let."

"your what?"

"My bracelet" he say pointing at a distance.

"I'll search for it later. Let me drop you to your room first."

"Nooooooooooo... I... want my brace....let," he slurs.

"Fine," I say, making him sit on a small table, and start searching for his bracelet. It takes me a total of 5 minutes to find it, but when I turn, Ishan is nowhere to be found.

The door of the room is locked from the outside, so he can't be inside.

"Ishan... where are you?" I shout. As I search for him, I notice a small back door that is slightly open.

I open it and look outside. Ishan is walking in the garden, his every footstep looks like he is on the verge of collapsing.

"Ishan, stop!" I shout and run towards him. He stops and looks in my direction, then starts walking again.

"What are you doing here?" I ask, huffing.

He doesn't say anything and just sits on the ground, tugging at my shirt to make me sit down too. His eyes are half open. He scans my facial features and starts poking my face with his index finger.

"What......a...shame....you... don't....have... pimples."

"Pimples" I ask confusedly.

"Yeah pimples....they appear when you smile"

"You mean dimples?"

"Yeah.... same....no.. difference."

"I do have dimples" I tell him

"I can't see them" he says looking at my cheeks.

"Yeah because I am not smiling"

"Then.....smile naa"

"Ehhh?"

"Please" he whines.

I have no other option so I fake smiles for him, he touches my dimple and smiles genuinely, "You look beautiful with them."

Then he touch his own cheek and his smile falters, "I don't have dimples...so sad" he say slowly more to himself than me.

"You know that dimples are a type of deformity?" He looks at me as if I have said something very offensive.

"Whatever" He says looking toward the sky

"We should go back" I offers and he just shake his head. "It's....better..... here"

A moment passed without any word he just keep admiring the beauty of night, he closes his eyes and open them again he do it 4-5 times as if he is struggling to keep them  open. "You are sleepy, let's go back now please"

"You know what there is this boy....ummm what's his name?" He speaks ignoring my plea completely.

"Sabnam?....no....shubham....no... Ahhhh what it was.... Shivam? Nah...." I never heard this many variations of my name. He holds his head in frustration. "What's his naaaaaammmeee?"

"Are you talking about Shubman?"

"Yeah Shubman.....by the way who keeps their name Shubman?, such a long name" well boy you are talking about my name infront of me..but nevermind.

"What  were you saying about Shubman?" I ask bringing him back on the topic.

"Yeah so... this boy sabnam.....he asked Mee to be his fake boyfriend" he is totally unaware of the fact that 'sabnam'is sitting right in front of him

"It's Shubman" I correct him again.

"Yeah yeah shubham........and I agreed to be his fake boyfriend....and ......then you know what happened?"

"What?" I ask knowing fully what actually happened after that.

~~~

Title: Chapter : 10
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

"It's Shubman," I correct him again.

"Yeah, yeah, Shubham... and I agree to be his fake boyfriend... and... then you know what happened?"

"What?" I ask, fully aware of what actually happened after that.

"Then someone pub.......lishes an......artisal (article) saying that we are dancing (dating) ........for real." The alcohol is working its way as his speech becomes more slurred. But what baffles me is that he remembers all this but doesn't remember that I am the one he is talking about right now.

"And this.....sab....nam....He didn't even bother to oppose the idea of fake dasing...I mean how can he agree to it that easily-I literally have to abandon my best friend for all this, aaarhhhh I was having a great vacation" I didn't bother to correct my name this time, and let him Butcher it mercilessly.He is literally whining like a five year old kid, this is what alcohol does to you.

He is sitting quite close to me, moving his head left and right as if he wants to find a comfortable position.

"And now........ we have to pretend..... as boy.......friends," he says, finally resting his head on my shoulder.

"And you are not okay with that?" I ask, nudging him a little.

He moves his head to look me in the eyes. "How.....do.... you ......know? Are you......psyco-"

"You mean psychic?"

He just hums in response.

"I just know." I reply, shrugging my shoulders.

He leans on my shoulder again and snuggles a little closer to me this time because the night winds are quite chilly.

After a moment, he asks, "Wait, who are you?" He jolts away, trying to sit straight.

"Ummm, I am Shubman."

"Shubman? You are Shubman too?"

"I guess so."

Now what Ishan is doing takes me by surprise; he cups my face and moves it left and right, squinting his eyes to get a better look.

"You are my, oh my, you are my boyfriend... uh, fake one," he asks, still cupping my face.

Instead of saying anything, I just nod my head slightly. He let go of my face and whispers,"Oooooopssss I just bad mouthed about you infront of you.....hihihi" and covers his mouth trying to  muffle his laugh.

And all of a sudden his expression becomes serious, "you.... know it's sooooooooooo........ wrong," and loses the last bit of consciousness he had.

Now I am sitting in the garden in the middle of the night with a drunk Ishan sleeping by my side, using my shoulder as support.

Life has been giving me surprises lately. I thought I'd be able to talk to Ishan, apologize to him, and make him understand. I indeed talked to Ishan, but a drunk one.

As I carry him to his room and lay him on the bed, and cover him with a blanket, he stirs in his sleep, saying something incoherent, and...

"OWWWWWHHHHHHH WHAT THE ACTUAL-............"

Have you ever heard of being manhandled by a drunk sleeping person? Well, that just happened to me. Ishan literally pulled my hand so hard that my forehead slammed against the wall behind.

I rub my forehead gently as it is paining a little and notice that Ishan is still holding my wrist while snoring lightly.

I try to remove my hand from his grasp, but he just makes some grunting sound in his sleep and tightens his hold.

WHAT THE HELL DOES HE WANT... ARGHHH LET ME SLEEP IN MY ROOM PEACEFULLY. WHEN I SAID YES TO TAKE HIM TO HIS ROOM, I DIDN'T SIGN UP FOR THIS.

With a lot of effort and struggle, I somehow manage to make some space for myself in the bed. He is still holding my hand.

I am trying to sleep, but the physical closeness between Ishan and me is kinda distracting. I don't sleep with anyone or share rooms with anyone. But here I am trying to sleep in a small space, my hand in Ishan's grip.

I notice his face, covered in shades of pink and red, his hair disheveled, his soft curls covering his forehead and his lips making a cute pout... Did I just say CUTE?

---

As I open my eyes, adjusting to the brightness, the memories of last night flood back. The last thing I remember before completely drifting to sleep is someone hugging me.

And that someone is none other than my fake boyfriend, Ishan Kishan.

Ishan Kishan se yaad aaya -- Where is he? I look around, but there's no sign of him in the room... he might have woken up early.

As I make my way towards the bathroom, I notice him lying on the floor, the blanket tangled around his leg. The bed is quite big, so how on earth did he end up there? His party clothes are all crumpled now, as if mine are any better.

When I come out after taking a shower, he is still lying in the same position.

"Ishan... Ishan... wake up."

No response.

I shake his shoulders a little.

Again No response.

"Please wake up," I say, closing off his nostrils.

"Ouch... owhh... Oowhhhh." A punch lands on my nose, not so subtly, by none other than your favorite Ishan Kishan. Tell me he din't intentionally do that because there's no way he hit me twice in his sleeping state.

And now he is finally awake, holding his head, unaware of the pain he just caused me.

"Aahhh, my head is spinning," he says hoarsely, his face morphing into an unpleasant expression.

And you just spun my nose. What about that? Huh?

"This hurts so bad," he says, pressing his head between his fingers. He is definitely having a bad hangover.

~~~

RCB WON THE WPL FINAL🥺❤️

Title: Chapter : 11
Chapter_Text:
•ISHAN KISHAN•

My head is literally exploding with pain; it feels like someone is hammering it.

"Aahhh, my head is spinning," I say to no one in particular.

Someone is hovering over me, but I don't know who; my vision is blurry, and opening my eyes fully hurts a lot.

"This hurts so bad," I try massaging my head.

"Wait," comes a voice from the person present in the room, but I can't recognize it, as my head is buzzing. Literally.

I can hear some rummaging noise, but I still don't bother to open my eyes.

Someone puts a pill in my mouth followed by a sip of water. I gulp it down, still not bothering to open my eyes. After a few seconds, someone lifts my head and puts it in their lap. My head rests against that person's thigh, and I feel a gentle pressure on my temples. I am getting a head massage.

While receiving the massage, I realize one thing: I am currently lying on a hard, cool floor. But how did I end up here? Also, why don't I remember anything from last night?

All I remember is chatting with Shreyas and Hardik bhai; everything after that is a blank memory. How I ended up sleeping on the floor is also a mystery.

The massage I'm receiving from the person in the room feels good, and the pain is subsiding.

After I don't know how long, the person speaks. "Does it feel any better?" This time, I recognize the voice. It's Shubman.

"Yeah, it feels better," I say, finally opening my eyes and looking at him.

"How did you get that?" I ask, pointing toward his forehead. There is a purple-ish bruise there, not to mention that my head is still on his lap.

He touches the bruise and hisses a little, indicating it might have pained. "This... ah, you won't believe me, but I got it because of you."

"Because of me?" I sit up, facing him, because having a conversation with my head in his lap is kind of awkward.

"Yeah."

"How?"

"You don't remember anything?"

Why is he saying it as if I've done something ominous?

"No, I don't. What happened last night?"

He sighs and starts narrating last night's events. "You got drunk last night. Shreyas asked me to drop you off at your room, but somehow you ended up in the garden. Then, you went on a rant there...."

Oh, don't tell me I did or said anything embarrassing.

He continues, "And this," he points towards his bruise, "In your sleep, you pulled my head with such force that I ended up banging my forehead on the wall."

"Seriously?" Whatever he's saying is quite unbelievable.

"Yeah."

"I'm sorry."

"And not just that, when I tried waking you up, you punched on my nose."

"I'm sorry, again."

"Were you dreaming about WWE?" He asks with a serious expression.

I ignore his question and ask, "But how did I end up on the floor?"

"I want to ask the same question. How did you end up here? You were on the bed with me"

"With you?"

"Did I forget to tell you that, you didn't let go of my hand, and even tightens your grip, so I had no choice but to sleep with you"

Okay, this is quite embarrassing. I banged his head, punched him on the nose, and also didn't let go of his hand. What must he be thinking about me?

But despite all this, he helps me and even gives me a head massage, which actually helps with the pain. So sweet of him, and now I feel like an asshole for ignoring him. I don't know whether to say thank you or sorry again.

"I am sorry and thank you," his expression softens. He gets up from the floor and gives me his hand. I take it and get up too.

"You should take a shower now; you'll feel good."

"Your massage helped a lot. Thank you, Shubman," I say sincerely.

He just looks at me with a slight smile and nods his head. After I come out from the shower, he is sitting on the bed, reading something on the phone. He looks up and asks, "Do you need lemonade or something?" I search and find that it helps with a hangover.

"Nope, I am all good now."

We both make our way to the hall area, where everyone is seated, someone is explaining them something. That someone is Nia and she is explaining the whole fake dating scenario to them. No one in the teams knew about it, but now they do.

Of course, the team has to know. Nia completes her speech, gives Shubman a quick acknowledging nod, and leaves.

As the whole thing registers in their brains, their expressions start changing - some become serious, some teasing, and some amused.

It looks like Surya, Yuzi, and Hardik bhai are seconds away from laughing their hearts out. And why not? If I were in their position, I would laugh too at the absurdity of the situation.

And I can sense the teasing coming in three... two... one -

"Look, here come the lovebirds," Bhuvi bhaiya starts.

"Yeah, yeah, THE IT COUPLE OF ICT," how can Yuzi bhaiya miss a chance to tease, huh? Especially when it comes to the juniors of the team.

"And guess what, Shubman was handling his drunk boyfriend yesterday," Hardik bhaiya announces with a teasing smile.

"Look at the height difference. Is it just a coincidence that they have such a cute height difference?" How can Shardul Bhai not say anything in this situation?

"Okay, okay, stop it now," Virat bhaiya protests in our favor.

"Vo naye naye couple hain Sharma jayenge." Phew! So much to expect from Virat bhaiya.

Everyone snickers and gives us teasing looks, and we just stand there, being the subject of their entertainment.

~~~

Me : It feels like I am moving on from the world cup heartbreak.

Instagram with that one well edited reel of 19th November heartbreak:

Khada hoon Aaj bhi wahi 🧍......

Title: Chapter : 12
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

After breakfast, everyone is chilling in the living room, some talking about Ishan and me, and some discussing the upcoming series against Sri Lanka.

Ishan is with Yuzi Bhai, and I am sitting a little away from them, but I can still hear them clearly.

"How I wish you both were dating for real," says Yuzi Bhai almost dreamily.

"Why so?" asks Ishan, with confusion.

"Because then we would be discussing how you are going to propose to Shubman, with the beach as a backdrop, and I'll be holding a poster which will say: 'Will you marry me, My Shubi?'"

Ishan practically cringes at it. I know Yuzi Bhai is just teasing him, but what if Ishan were my real boyfriend? What would it be like?

But that's not possible; he is straight, and it's not that I want to be in a relationship with him. I am happy being single and not ready to mingle. Thank you very much.

---

The week passes in a blur, and now we are in Dharamshala for our first ODI against Sri Lanka. Did I mention that it's my ODI debut as well?

I received my debut cap from none other than Rohit bhaiya, in the presence of Mom, Dad, and Sheen, for which I am grateful. I knew I was in the squad, but I didn't know I'd receive my ODI debut cap in the first match itself. Everyone congratulates me, and I feel grateful to be a part of this team, but I also feel nervous.

To my surprise, Darshan is here as well with Nia. They both congratulate me. "I want at least fifty today," it's her way of wishing me luck. "Why not a hundred?" asks Darshan, furrowing an eyebrow towards her.

"Because I don't want to put pressure on him, you know, the pressure of expectations," she says dramatically. Honestly, I have a better bond with Nia than with Darshan. She is a fun girl to have around.

"And how's your dating life going, Mr. Gill?" she asks with a mischievous smile. Ah, I can't blame her for being this frank with me; I mean, I am the one who has given her this freedom. And also, we have one thing in common: the fear of Darshan, but she handles him way better. Not gonna lie.

Everyone is in the dressing room, getting ready as the match is about to start, when Ishan comes to me and says, "Congratulations, Shubman," with a polite smile. "Thank you," I reply.

"So, feeling excited to play your first international ODI?"

"Yeah, but nervous as well."

"It's very natural to feel that way. I was literally vomiting an hour before my debut. You are doing so much better," I know he is trying to ease me.

"I didn't sleep last night because of the excitement," I tell him.

"It's okay, just believe in yourself. You'll do great today," he says, patting my shoulder before going away to get ready.

After that night, he is being easy with me and not ignoring me, which is good, but we still cannot call each other friends - more or less, we are still each other's teammates. He has a way better bond with other members, not that I am jealous of it.

As Ishan and I step on the ground to open for Team India, the whole crowd starts cheering in our support, raising the Indian flag and waving their posters.

I see some posters congratulating me for my debut, and then I see some others with my and Ishan's picture pasted on them, with something written on it. I couldn't read it because of the distance, but whatever it is, it's positive.

There are chants of "Ishan, Ishan."

There are chants of "Shubman, Shubman."

And then there are other chants which go like this: "Ishman, Ishman."

It takes me a minute to realize that they have merged our names to make "Ishman" with it. "ISH" from Ishan's name and "MAN" from my name.

I knew that our dating news is getting a lot of traction online, but what I am seeing here is quite unbelievable.

Standing here and witnessing this makes me realize that whatever we have done really has a huge impact on the public.

I look towards Ishan. His expressions are showcasing that he is also realizing what I just realized.

The game started, and I am on 36 runs when I try hitting a six. But two fielders are there to take the catch. I thought that's it, only 36 runs in the debut match, but fortunately, both weren't able to take the catch. It was a funny scene to watch because the catch was quite easy, but somehow both the fielders messed it up.

I look towards Ishan, and we both start laughing. The crowd is also not able to contain their laughs. The mis-fielding was actually hilarious; it's like they are giving competition to the Pakistanis in mis-fielding.

I complete my maiden ODI 50, and the feeling is surreal. I raise my bat towards my parents only to realize that Nia is gesturing something to me.

She is asking me to hug Ishan. Why? Because we are boyfriends in public, and they do hug at an achievement.

Obeying Nia, I go towards Ishan and hug him. Not the teammate wala hug, but more like - *OMG, I am so happy I just made my maiden 50, let me just hug my boyfriend* wala hug.

As I hug Ishan, he stiffens but quickly realizes that it's just for the show and hugs me back. I look towards Nia; she is literally cheering.

---

Our next match is in Ahmedabad. When we enter the hotel, Rohit bhaiya has news to share. "From now on, two players will be sharing the same room." This news kind of comes as a surprise to everyone.

There are murmurs between the players. "And the pairings are," Virat bhaiya starts naming the pairs, "Surya and Shreyas, Rohit and Hardik (🗿), Bhuvi and Jaddu, Rahuliya and miyaan, me and Yuzi, jassi and shardul, jaddu and kuliya......." He continues, ".......Gill and Ishan."

"What?" questions Ishan.

"What? What? You two are pretending to be boyfriends, so it's only reasonable to give you two the same room," says Virat bhaiya sternly.

"And also, what if we pair you two with someone else, and someone from the staff breaks this to the media?" reasons Rohit bhaiya.

"Right, we can't afford more rumors about the ICT; we already have enough on our plate," Jaddu Bhai chimes in.

"Do you have any problem with the room arrangements?" asks Surya Bhai to Ishan.

Ishan just makes an uninterested face. Surya Bhai then looks at me for an answer "No, I don't have any problem." Where is my self-respect when I need some?

But why is Ishan being a bitch about sharing the room with me? Huh? We were good today, didn't we?

Everyone goes to their assigned rooms. As I enter mine, Ishan is already there, searching for something in his suitcase. He looks at me for a second and then continues his search.

After a whole ten minutes of nerve-wracking silence, I speak. "See, I am sorry about this whole thing. I didn't know that a little lie would blow out on our faces like this."

"I am not mad at you, Shubman. I thought about it and realized that it wasn't your mistake and whatever we are doing is the only option that we have."

"But you were ignoring me earlier, weren't you?"

"But I didn't ignore you today, did I?"

"Then what's the problem now? Why are you being awkward here? You don't want to share the room with me?"

"It's not like that."

"Then what is it?"

What he replies makes me giggle.

"Ummm, actually..." He hesitates a little. "I thought sharing a room with you wouldn't be a good idea because I have already embarrassed myself in my sleep previously," he said, referring to the night when he was drunk.

"So you don't want to embarrass yourself more in front of me?" I ask, giggling.

He just looks down, embarrassed. Okay, he is really cute.

"You don't have to be, Ishan. You were drunk; it's okay," I say genuinely. "And look, we have a couch here, and I can sleep on it."

"No, I'll sleep on the couch. You can take the bed," he protests.

Before I could say anything, Surya Bhai enters the room. "Oho, look at you two taking care of each other, huh? And why does anyone need to sleep on the couch when you have a huge bed right there?"

~~~

While writing this, I literally forgot why Ishan wasn't willing to share the room with Shubman.😭

Also some drama is going to unfold in the upcoming chapters, which will kind of bring them closer emotionally. I'm excited to write that, and hopefully, it will turn out the way I want it to.

One more thing, I created Darshan's character just to annoy Shubman 🗿💅

How are you liking the story so far?(Koi bezzati nahi karega)

Title: Chapter : 13
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

"You don't have to be, Ishan. You were drunk; it's okay," I say genuinely. "And look, we have a couch here, and I can sleep on it."

"No, I'll sleep on the couch. You can take the bed," he protests.

Before I can say anything, Surya Bhai enters the room. "Oho, look at you two taking care of each other, huh? And why does anyone need to sleep on the couch when you have a huge bed right there?"

Ishan tries to say something but Surya Bhai cuts him off. "And what were you two talking about, the embarrassment?"

"So you were eavesdropping on us?" I raise my eyebrows.

"Naah, I just happened to overhear that. Don't divert the topic, tell me about the 'embarrassment,' who embarrassed whom?" He asks curiously.

I start with "Actually Ishan....." in a teasing tone. The moment I say this, Ishan's eyes go a little wide, and he looks toward me with pleading eyes, as if asking me not to tell anything to Surya Bhai.

".......nothing mentionable," I say, dismissing the topic, and can sense Surya Bhai's disappointment. Sadly, now he has one less topic to tease about.

"So, one of you is going to sleep on the couch?" He looks alternately between me and Ishan. And we both don't say anything.

So Surya Bhai continues, "I mean, you two are adults and can sleep on one bed without any action involved, you know!" He completes with a playful wink.

Ishan plops down beside him and in a whining manner says, "Tell me that one day this teasing thing is going to end."

"NEVER," says Surya Bhai while throwing a cushion on Ishan's face. And just like that, they are having a cushion fight, and I am being ignored completely.

After a while, when Surya Bhai leaves, leaving us alone in the privacy of our room, we both get ready as we have an evening practice session today.

We return back to our rooms. I am slouching on the couch, scrolling mindlessly through the phone when I notice Ishan come and stand in front of me.

I look at him, and he stretches his hand for a handshake. I raise one eyebrow, not knowing what he is doing.

"Ah, we are roommates now, and also fake boyfriends, and I don't know what we have to share in the near future, so it's only good that we stop being awkward around each other and be friends?"

I smile at him.

"So, friends?" he asks.

"Friends!" I reply, taking his stretched hand and shaking gently, feeling his soft, warm hands.

"Lets sleep on the bed only, Surya Bhai was right, it's big enough for both of us," he says, pointing toward the bed.

"Of course, we are both adults and can sleep together without any action involved," I say, mimicking Surya Bhai, and he chuckles.

"And of course, we have slept together before as well."

'Or, more accurately, you forced me to in your drunken state' I thought but refrain from saying aloud.

In the night when everyone was going to bed, we heard some commotion outside. It turns out there is some kind of argument going on in Rohit bhaiya's room, and other team members are also there.

"Haan tu Abcd se hi baat krle, meri neend gyi tel lene," Rohit bhaiya says with a 'I am very done with you cheeku' face.

["Yeah, you just talk to Abcd, what about my sleep?"]

"Pehli baat toh yeh that it's ABD and not ABCD," Virat bhaiya corrects him.

["First of all its ABD and not ABCD"]

"Haan wohi," as if he cares whether it's ABD or ABCD.

["Yeah same"]

"Kya ho gya Rohit bhai?" asks a confused Shreyas, not knowing what's actually going on.

["What happened Rohit bro?"]

"Arrey dekh na ye apni dusri biwi se kabse baat kiye Jaa rha aur mujhe sone nhi de rha," by 'Dusri biwi,' (second wife) Rohit bhaiya meant AB de Villiers.

["Look, he's been talking to his second wife for so long and not letting me sleep."]

"Haan toh, we are talking after so long," Virat bhaiya defends himself.

["So what? We are talking after so long."]

Jaddu bhaiya, being the sensible one, asks a valid question: "Pr Rohit ki room pairing to Hardik ke saath thi na?"

[But Rohit's room pairing was with Hardik no?"]

"Na baba na, Isko Virat hi jhel skta hain, so I changed it with Virat and also his snoring is insufferable," Hardik bhaiya voices his concern, who I didn't even know was here because he was standing at the door.

["Nah man, Only Virat can handle him, so I changed it with virat and also his snoring is insufferable"]

Rohit bhaiya glares at Hardik Bhai, with a look that says: 'Tujhe toh beta Mai batata hoon.'

"Pr mai ye Virat ko nhi jhel skta, pata nhi kitni baat karni hoti hain isko ABCD se."

["But I can't tolerate Virat. I don't know how much he has to talk to ABCD."]

"Kya mtlb Rohit bhaiya aap jealous ho rhe ho AB bhai se?" It's Yuzi bhaiya who teases him.["What do you mean, Rohit bhaiya, are you getting jealous of AB bro?"]

"Aye Yuzi tu jyada woh Mat ban," now Rohit bhai's glare shifts to Yuzi Bhai.

[ Don't act like that yuzi"]

"Shana," Virat bhaiya tells us what Rohit bhaiya's 'woh' ('that') means.["smart"]

"Ishu tu mere saath so ja," he looks towards Ishan with hope.["Ishu, come sleep with me,"]

Ishan, while taking a step back, says "umm bhaiya I am good aur vaise bhi, mai toh pure bed pr ghumta hoon sote huye so-...."

["Umm, bro, I'm good. Besides, I usually roll around the entire bed while sleeping, so..."]

"Abbey saale Tera boyfriend kya bana apne bhaiya ko bhul gya," Rohit bhaiya says, faking his anger.

["Hey, you jerk, now that you've a boyfriend, you've forgotten about your bro."]

After another 20 minutes of argument, it's settled that Virat bhaiya will share the room with Rohit bhaiya and Virat bhaiya will talk less with ABD, and now everyone is dismissed.

While returning to our room, I ask him, "Is it true what you were saying about your sleeping habit?"

"No, not really," he says nonchalantly.

In the room, we both settle on the bed, me on the right and him on the left, or let's say on the 'far-left' because it seems like he is one push away from falling onto the floor.

Tonight, I realize that whatever Ishan was saying about his sleeping habit is actually true because, after dozing off, from the far left side, he is now lying just beside me.

And in the morning, I realize one more thing: a sober Ishan is much more clingy than a drunken one as he is currently snuggling up to me, but for some reason, it doesn't feel weird.

---

In the second ODI, India won, thanks to Mii's (Miyan) bowling attack. It's an easy win, and the highlight was Virat bhaiya's catch, which seemed impossible, but it's Virat bhaiya, and he made it possible. We couldn't stop talking about it on our way back to the hotel.

As we both make our way to the room, Nia and Darshan are standing there, waiting for us.

"You two here?" I ask.

"We want to talk about something," replies Nia.

"Oh, what happened?"

All four of us settle ourselves in the room.

"There is negative PR going around you, Shubman," Darshan speaks with seriousness. Well, he is always serious, so nothing new.

"And not just about you but about your relationship with Ishan too," Nia adds.

"What kind of negative PR?"

"That you and Ishan broke up and also that you don't have a friendly relationship with the team and stuff like that," Nia tells me.

"Oh, but why are there rumors like that? I mean, there is nothing that triggered those rumors," I ask, a valid question.

To which Darshan explains, "It's their job to generate headlines that attract an audience. Also, big names whose sons didn't able to make their place in ICT also tend to do negative PR against BCCI or players."

"Yeah, and we already had enough of those, but your dating thing kind of outpowered those rumors," Nia says.

"If it's also getting  negative publicity, then shouldn't we just stop this dating thing?" I say, trying to be logical.

"NOOOOO, ARE YOU MAD?" says an offended Nia as if I have said something illegal.

"And why so?"

"Do you seriously think that doing this will make these negative PR things stop? In fact, there will be more. People will think that ICT is homophobic, that's why you two have to break up, and also, it will affect your and Ishan's individual image as well."

During this whole conversation, Ishan didn't speak at all. He is just sitting here, keenly listening to us.

"So what's next? What's the plan? Do we have to get married or something?" One should not use sarcasm when Darshan is there.

"We are thinking of sending you two on a date or two to send a message that your relationship is fine." States Darshan completely  ignoring my sarcasm.

"Do we really need to do that?" I pretends to be annoyed

"Yup" anwers Nia casually.

"When?" This is the first word that Ishan speaks.

Nia's head snaps towards him. She gives him a lopsided smile and in a teasing tone says, "Are you eager to go on a date with him?"

When I look toward Ishan, did... did he just... did he just blush? Like a little? Or did I imagine that?

I am staring at Ishan when he also looks toward me, and I just divert my eyes.

"Not now, otherwise, it will look deliberate." Says Darshan stating the obvious.

"Also, the ODI series is going on, so now is not the correct time, probably after a week or so because we don't want people to think that it's a deliberate attempt to make everything look normal." Remarks nia.

"And we came here to tell you not to make any stupid mistakes. If people get to know that it's all fake, then all hell will break loose," Darshan's says with a warning tone.

All three of them looking at me as if I am the only one who is prone to making stupid mistakes. Not that I disagree with them but still making me feel it is very rude of them.

With that warning, they both left, leaving me and Ishan looking at each other, not knowing what to say.

~~~

Title: Chapter : 14
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

Five days later, against my better judgment, I am getting ready for the fake date. If you actually consider putting on a T-shirt and cargo pants "getting ready," then sure.

When I look at him, I feel underdressed. He wears a denim jacket over a plain T-shirt and trousers, and not just that, he has also styled himself with accessories like a pearl necklace and a watch, and those earrings he always wears—they suit him, in my opinion.

He is dressed, and me? I am dressed (if you call it dressing) like someone going out to buy groceries.

We are currently in the car with Nia and Darshan, not knowing exactly where we are going.

And I am feeling like a fool in front of him. Thank God I at least decided to wear shoes instead of flip-flops. The only good thing is that none of them seems to judge my outfit.

Nia breaks the long silence, "You two don't have to do anything extraordinary," she looks at me and then at Ishan. "You just have to eat together, and we will make sure that the paparazzi get the pictures."

Fake relationship, fake boyfriend, fake dates—the word "fake" sums up my current life quite accurately.

The car stops outside a very fancy restaurant. As soon as we enter, I know it's Darshan's choice because, even though it's fancy, it's equally boring.

We both settle at our reserved table, trying to act like normal human beings, which we are. Ishan seems more chill than me. He is always more chill than me.

The food is served. If we were in a less fancy restaurant, the food might have been tasty, because seriously, I don't want to disrespect food, but what is this?

In an attempt to make small talk, I say, "Your pearl necklace looks good on you."

He touches it, "Yeah, thanks. Actually, I like pearls."

This conversation is followed by silence, during which we pick at our tasteless food, trying not to make faces.

"If we are here on the pretext of a date, why don't we try to get to know each other?" he suggests.

"Okay, so what's your favorite color?"

He looks at me with a small smile playing on his lips, "Shubman, you can do better than this, trust me!" His statement is laced with sarcasm, but his tone is genuine, so I don't take offense.

"Yeah, I agree." I seriously don't have any self respect.

Then, after a moment of thinking, he asks, "A famous book that you haven't read yet." I can see the curiosity in his eyes.

"The answer won't please you," I say, taking a spoonful of whatever we are eating.

"Tell me, I won't judge you."

"Literally every famous book."

His spoon stops in mid-air momentarily, his expression turning from curious to disappointed. "Really?"

"You said you won't judge me," I say, making a pout.

"I am not judging you."

"Yeah, I can see that."

To which he just shakes his head a little, smiling, and resumes eating his food, as if telling me, "I didn't expect any better from you."

Thank goodness I am not in a real relationship with him.

"Tell me your favorite book," I ask, because I now know he is interested in books.

He tells me the name of a book which I've never heard of. In my defense, I haven't heard much about books, so yeah.

The only books I've heard about are the Harry Potter series and the fact that those films were based on books, which I found out not so long ago.

So the conclusion is that I really know jackshit about books.

It's a shame.

Not for me, though.

After a while, he looks at me and asks, "Why are you being extra careful?"

"I don't want to end up in the headlines for the wrong reasons."

"What kind of headlines?"

" *Young cricketer splashes food over boyfriend, was it his attempt to break up with boyfriend*,

or

*The future of Indian cricket does not have any future with his boyfriend, young cricketer jeopardizes his own date*,

or or

*Ten things you didn't know about this young Indian cricketer, the number ten will shock you*."

He lets out a throaty laugh at my statement, and after that, the ice between us breaks, and we talk about a lot of things like and dislikes and almost everything.

It makes me realize that Ishan is indeed an interesting person; being friends with him would be really nice.

After we are done with our food and the dessert, which is surprisingly good compared to the tasteless food, my phone rings, and Nia's name flashes on it.

"You two are done; now you can go home in the car," she says directly, coming to the point.

"Okay, wait a minute, how do you know that we are done?" I scan the place only to realize that she and Darshan are a few tables away from us. Nia waves at me.

"So you two are spying on us."

"No."

"You are."

"Darshan said that we needed to be here to see if you end up screwing the whole thing."

"I am offended."

"He was right."

"But I haven't done anything stupid."

"But you could have."

"Ugh, so what was it? A double date?"

"You can call it that."

"Wait a damn minute, you just called him Darshan and not Darshan sir. Is there something going on between you two?"

"I don't know."

"I need a yes or no."

"I don't know. Now go, you don't talk to anyone this long when you are on a date. Now be a gentleman and drop him off."

I glare at her, and she just cuts the call and gets busy talking with Darshan. There is definitely something going on.

"What happened, and why they are here?" he ask pointing at their direction.

"Nothing" I say getting up "we should go now, I will drop you."

In the car, he puts on some music. This date is yielding fruitful results for me, or for us. Honestly, I am much more comfortable with him now; the silence between us no longer feels awkward.

We are progressing in our friendship, which is a good thing.

I stop the car in front of his apartment, and he hops off. As I am about to leave, he comes and opens the car door, offering me a hand with a wide smile. "Come."

Um, excuse me? Did I miss something, or did I skip soms chapters? What's happening? Why is he inviting me to his house out of nowhere with that expression?

Someone help please !

~~~

Title: Chapter : 15
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

I stop the car in front of his house, and he hops out. As I am about to leave, he comes and opens the car door, offering me a hand with a wide smile. "Come."

Um, excuse me? Did I miss something, or did I skip some chapters? What's happening? Why is he inviting me to his house out of nowhere with that expression?

Someone, help please!

As I contemplate why he is all of a sudden behaving like this, he comes close to my face - ummm?

His face is so close to mine that I can feel his warm breath. He then whispers in my ear, "My mom is here."

"Huh?" I look at him and then on the other side, a woman is standing there looking at us.

"So?" I ask him, not knowing why he stopped me.

"Please come with me. After the article incident, I haven't talked to her properly. If you come with me, then I won't have to listen to her lectures."

"So you didn't know that she was coming?"

"No, she didn't tell me."

Should I help him? Or should I make some excuse and leave? I want to choose the latter option, but I am not an asshole.

Whom am I kidding? I really am an asshole.

"Umm, Ishan, um...," Before I could complete my sentence, he tightly holds my wrist. "Please, Shubman."

If I deny his request now, then I'll achieve expert level of assholeness, and I am in no mood to achieve that, at least not right now, so I agree to go with him.

As we approach her, Ishan gives her a tight hug. "You didn't tell me that you were coming."

She breaks the hug and asks, "Oh, so that you can make some excuse? Tell me, why haven't I met your boyfriend? And not just that, you didn't even tell me anything properly?"

"Ah, sorry, mom, actually-......"

His mom approaches me as I am standing a few steps away from them. She puts one hand on my shoulder. "Shubman, beta, you look even more handsome in real life."

And after she says that, I forget how to behave, how to smile, and even how to exist. What do you do when your boyfriend's mom compliments you? Do you say thank you? That's so arrogant.

Or do you just smile? But not saying anything is rude.

Why hasn't anyone told me what to do in such a situation? Yes, I need help in these situations because I am as awkward as hell in front of new people, especially when I have to present myself decently before them.

I look at Ishan, and he is doing something with his eyes. It takes me a moment to realize that he is asking me to touch her feet.

Yeah, I forgot basic manners/sanskars as well. Please don't judge me.

I touch her feet, and she puts her hand on my head. She takes both of us to the backyard, as the sun has already dipped, and the weather is very pleasant outside.

I keep looking at Ishan as if asking him to tell me what to say next or what to do next.

I can bet my gold chain that he is internally screaming, "Don't embarrass me, Shubman, please behave."

But only if I had his calm.

There should be classes where they tell you how to behave and what to say in these situations. More importantly, they should tell you how to behave in front of your fake boyfriend's mom.

"You know what, beta? He didn't tell me anything about you, and I got to know about your relationship through social media," she voices her disappointment.

But wait a minute, why didn't Ishan tell her that it's all fake? I will ask him later.

"I really wanted to meet you since then," she looks at me smiling and adoringly.

"Yeah, me too, aunty. I wanted to meet you. I even asked Ishan for it," lies, lies, lies.

She serves us drinks even after I declined.

"Have something, beta," I don't want to be rude, but I know for sure if I pick up the glass, I'll either end up breaking it or spilling the drink, or worse, spilling the drink over Ishan. I am in no mood to do any of it and embarrass myself.

"For how long are you going to stay here?" Ishan asks in an attempt to divert the topic from the drink.

"Why aren't you happy that I came?" He asks dramatically.

"NO, NO, THAT'S NOT WHAT I MEANT," he says defensively. "I asked because you came alone and not with dad."

"I am going the day after tomorrow. I came here to attend one of my friend's daughter's wedding," she explains.

"Ohh."

"Shubman, do you want to see Ishan's childhood pictures?" There is excitement in the way she asks me, as if she was only waiting for Ishan to have a boyfriend so that she can show him Ishan's childhood pictures.

"Sure, aunty," as soon as I say this, Ishan looks at me as if I have said something wrong.

"Mom, no, please," he whines to her.

"What, no? Let me show him your cute childhood pictures," she says, getting up to get the album.

"Yeah, cute and embarrassing pictures," he says to her as she walks away.

Once she was completely inside the house, Ishan asks, "Why didn't you deny her?"

"Why should I? And also, she is so excited to show me those."

He just makes a grimace expression and says, "For the next hour, you are going to see a show whose title is: Mom embarrassing her own son in front of his boyfriend, just for fun."

I giggle as he dramatically finishes his speech, and he just glares at me, not so harshly.

Now I am feeling much more comfortable here. His mom returns with a huge photo album in her hand.

She starts showing me the pictures, and with each picture, she also tells the backstory of that picture.

"And this..." She laughs, showing me a picture of Ishan in which he is wearing nothing, literally nothing.

"He was refusing to wear anything, God knows why, and Raj took the picture."

I too laugh with her.

"Oh god, mom, I wasn't even one at that time," and he just hides his face under his palm.

"And this one, do you see how scared he looks here?" She shows the picture to Ishan as well.

"You won't believe, Shubman, but Ishan was so scared of cameras, he literally started crying every time he saw one."

I giggle and ask him, "Really, Ishan? Scared of cameras?"

"I WAS A FREAKING CHILD, OH MY GOD, I CAN'T EMBARRASS MYSELF ANYMORE HERE, I AM GOING INSIDE," he says loudly before barging inside the house. Poor Ishan, but I am enjoying this, hehe.

She then shows me some other pictures, and I have to say this: he was cute, and he is cute. I think he looks even cuter now, but will I ever tell him that? Naah.

She somehow manages to make me stay for dinner, even though Ishan wasn't very happy about that. Of course, he wasn't.

Ishan and I are sitting side by side. She serves us the yummy food and looks at us with those same heart eyes that my mom was giving us in the restaurant.

"You two look so good together," she says directly, staring at us with those heart eyes, and Ishan starts coughing.

I pass him the water, patting his back.

Now the look on his mom's face intensifies even more, and I am so sure that she is planning our marriage in her head.

After dinner, Ishan manages to drag me out of the house before his mom can practically make us marry each other on the spot.

"Your mom is really sweet," I say to him as we both approach the main gate.

"I know, right? But she can be overwhelming sometimes."

"Yeah, just like my mom. They could be really good friends."

We both stand beside the car, the cool breeze of the night feeling good on our skin.

"I forgot to ask, but why didn't you tell her that we aren't actually boyfriends?"

He leans against the car and replies, "If she and dad knew anything, it means the world knows it... They can't keep secrets, so it's better not to tell them anything."

"Ohhh," is all I could say.

My phone rings, and it's mom.

"Yes, mom... Huh?... Ishan?... Okay... Sure," the call ends within a minute.

"What happened?" asks Ishan because the conversation between mom and me includes his name as well.

"We have this big Pooja coming on Tuesday, so she asked me to invite you... You are free that day, right?"

"Yeah, I will come."

And as I was about to open the door of the car, I turned to him and teased him, "You really were scared of the cameras, weren't you... Are you still scared..."

He turns in my direction but somehow loses his balance, and in an attempt to save him from falling, I lose my balance too, and we both end up on the ground, Ishan under me and I over him.

He tightly shuts his eyes, but fortunately, my hand was over his head, so it didn't hurt him that much. He slowly opens his eyes and looks at mine, and... and...

"Shubman... Shubman."

"Huh... yeah."

"Where did you get lost? You were literally staring at me for 2 minutes. Can we get up now?"

"Ah-umm yeah, sure."

In an attempt to get up fast, I end up bumping my head on Ishan's.

"Owwwwhhhh," he cries.

"Oh my god, I am so sorry," I gently rub his forehead.

"It's okay," he says, but he lets me rub his forehead, and then he just starts laughing.

"Who would have thought that this day would end up like this," he says between his laughs.

"You mean us ending up on the ground?"

He just laughs, a melodic one, sweet and pleasant. He leans towards me while laughing and keeps his head over my shoulder.

And it feels...

Good.

Peaceful.

---

I am tired as I have been running errands since morning. Today, we have the big Pooja that I mentioned. It will start in the evening, but all the work needs to be done before that.

As the evening approaches, the guests start arriving. I was given the duty to welcome them, and I am least suitable for that duty.

Kuldeep bhaiya and Shreyas also arrive, as they were in Delhi, so I invited them as well.

Within an hour, all the guests have arrived except one: Ishan.

At first, I thought he might be late.

But now it's getting much later... I tried calling him, but he didn't pick up.

And I am not getting a good feeling about it. Even Kuldeep bhaiya and Shreyas tried calling him, but no response.

He told me that he'd arrive at 5, but now it's 8. If he got busy with something, he could have at least told me.

And now it's concerning.

~~~

Title: Chapter : 16
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

He said he'd arrive at 5, but it's almost 7 now. If he got busy with something, he could have at least informed me.

And now it's becoming concerning.

Why isn't he picking up my calls?

I anxiously glance at the clock every few minutes. I've been waiting for him for almost two hours now.

Ishan isn't usually this reckless. If he wasn't planning to come, he would have called me. I've called him numerous times, but there's been no response.

The Pooja has already started, but I couldn't bring myself to be there. I'm standing at the main gate, hoping for him to arrive. Every minute is nerve-wracking now.

As more time passes, my worries are turning into fears. My mind is jumping on worst case scenarios and I hate it.

I can't wait any longer. I've tried reaching his brother, but he's currently not in India, and his mom returned to Mumbai yesterday. So now, there's only one option—I'm going to search for him.

As I'm about to head inside to grab my keys, a car stops in front of the house. I glance at the car and realize it's Ishan's.

THANK FUCK.

He opens the car door and stands there, relief washing over me as I rush to him, momentarily forgetting my worries.

Reaching out to him, I say, "You're finally here. What took you so long? I've been waiting for over two hours, and you weren't picking up my calls," all in one breath.

But as I step closer, I notice that his demeanor has changed. His clothes are crumpled, his hair disheveled, and he's wearing sunglasses at night.

"And why—" I begin to ask about his condition, which doesn't look great, but he starts fixing his clothes and hair. His expressions are grim.

"What happened, Ishan? Are you okay?" My concern is quite evident in my voice.

He slightly nods his head. "Can I use the washroom?" he asks, ignoring my questions.

"Yeah, sure," I reply. I want to ask again what happened to him, but I think that can wait. I show him the way to my room.

When he comes out of the washroom, he has washed his face, and the sunglasses are gone. Then I notice his eyes, why do they look red and puffy as if he cried? He is not at all looking like his usual self. And now I am getting more worried.

"Ishan..." he cuts me off, as if he knows what I am going to ask.

In a low voice, he says, "We should join the Pooja now, otherwise we will miss it."

It seems like he is deliberately ignoring my questions.

"If you're not feeling good, then you can rest—"

"No, I'm good, I'm fine," he says in a manner that feels like he's not telling me but convincing himself.

If he doesn't want to tell me, I don't want to force it.

"Okay, let's go."

During all the time we were there, Ishan did not interact with anyone, which is very unlikely for him.

Even Kuldeep bhaiya and Shreyas notices the change too. When they ask me, I tell them that nothing serious was going on because I don't want to worry them, and also because I myself didn't know what is wrong actually.

As the whole thing wraps up, I notice Ishan's absence. At first, I thought he was gone, but I find him in the backyard just standing there. I can't see his face, so I walk over to him. "Ishan," I say softly. He turns slightly to acknowledge my presence, and then I notice that he is crying.

He hastily tries to wipe his tears and compose himself. "I should go now, it's late," he says as he tries to walk away. I hold his wrist, stopping him. "What happened, Ishan? You can tell me."

He looks into my eyes, his eyes welling up with tears. He looks so vulnerable; it breaks my heart to see him like this. He didn't say anything, just stood there. And I am still holding his wrist.

"Okay, you don't want to tell me, fine. But please stay for the night; you are not in a condition to drive."

And also, I don't want him to be alone. I desperately want him to stay.

He agrees to stay.

I hand him my clothes to change into, after changing he reappears from the washroom. He just stands there without saying anything. I stand up; I want to ask again why he was crying.

The air between us grows heavier with unspoken words. He looks so small and defeated. I want to reach out and pull him into a tight comforting embrace and assure him that whatever it is, it's going to be okay.

But he remains stubbornly distant, retreating further into himself, not wanting to reach out for any kind of help.

Eventually defeated, I reluctantly let the matter drop. Atleast for now.

"You can sleep here," I say, arranging the pillows. He doesn't say anything.

I receive a phone call, so I leave the room. When I return, I see him lying in bed all curled up, looking so small, his eyes closed. I pull the comforter over him.

His unkempt hair covers his forehead, so I gently pull them back.

As I'm about to leave, he grabs my hand. I look down at his hold and then at him. His eyes are still closed, and his hold is very gentle.

"Please stay," his voice barely above a whisper. If I weren't looking at his lip movement, I would have missed it.

I can sense his vulnerability.

"Okay," I say in a gentle tone. I don't know what happened or what he is going through, but one thing I know for sure is that I have to be here with him.

I lay beside him in bed. I can hear soft sniffling; he is crying again, probably, as his eyes were already red and puffy.

He is facing the other side."Come here," I say, shifting a little towards him. He turns himself towards me; he doesn't open his eyes, and just hugs me. I gently wrap my arms around him, pulling him closer, keeping his head pressed to my chest. I can feel his trembling breath against my skin.

Maybe he just needs this, needs some comfort at this moment, and I am willing to provide it. I want to ask again why he is crying, what probably happened, but I don't want to push him.

After a while, his hold tightens around me as he snuggles closer. I can feel the fabric of my t-shirt getting wet as he cries, silently this time.

"It's going to be okay," I murmur, tracing gentle circles on his back.

He lets out a shaky sigh, his body relaxing against my touch. I place a kiss on his head. I keep caressing his back gently, assuring him that whatever it is, it's going to be okay.

It aches my heart to see him like this.

Broken.

A few moments pass, and then he lifts his head slightly to look at me in the eye. His eyes are more puffy now. In an almost unheard whisper, he says, "Thank you."

"Shhh, it's okay, now sleep," he again presses his head against my chest, snuggling more into me.

We sleep like that, hugging each other.

---

The next morning when I open my eyes, I find the other side of the bed empty; Ishan is gone.

After getting ready, I ask Mom, "When did Ishan leave?"

"He left quite early. I asked him to stay for breakfast, but he denied, saying he had to reach somewhere," she replies.

"Oh, did he tell you where he is going?"

"No, but he seems quite sad. Is everything okay?"

"Yeah, yeah, everything's fine."

I call Ishan twice, but there's no response.

I call Raj bhaiya, hoping that maybe Ishan had told him something, but that's in vain because Ishan hasn't talked to his brother since yesterday morning.

"What happened, Shubman? Everything is fine, right? Even I called him today, but he didn't answer."

"Yes, bhaiya, everything is fine. He probably kept it on silent. I'll let you know once I reach him. Don't worry."

After hanging up the call, I drive straight to Ishan's house, but no one is there. As I get back in my car, dialing Ishan's number for the fifth time today, he picks up the call.

"Oh, God, Ishan, where are you? Do you have any idea how worried I am? You just left without saying anything," I say in one breath, not letting him speak.

Then, after a brief pause, he says, "I am in the hospital."

"What?"

~~~

Title: Chapter : 17
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

As I return to my car, dialing Ishan's number for the fifth time today, he picks up the call.

"Oh, God, Ishan, where are you? Do you have any idea how worried I am? You just left without saying anything," I say in one breath, not letting him speak.

After a brief pause, he says, "I am in the hospital."

"What? What are you doi-... oh my god, wait, are you okay? Did anything happen to you?" The panic in my voice is rising with every word.

"No, I am fine."

"Then why did you---- ...tell me which hospital. I am coming there."

He tells me the name, and I am currently 15 minutes away from that hospital.

The good thing is that Ishan is safe, or at least it seems so from his call.

But I am still very anxious, so I drive as fast as I can.

As I reach the hospital, he is standing there in the lobby, still wearing my clothes.

I practically jog to him. "Are you seriously okay?" I ask, checking his arms for any injuries.

"Calm down, Shubman, I already told you I am fine."

My worries turn into frustration. "Yeah, you told me how fine you were. Oh my god, did I forget to mention how absolutely stunning you looked yesterday? And today, how glad I was to find out that you were gone and no one knows anything about your whereabouts, and on top of all this, His Royal Highness decides to keep his phone on silent so his peasant can't reach him." I finish my little sarcastic monologue, and he starts smiling. THE AUDACITY.

But one thing that doesn't go unnoticed is that he looks much better, not as miserable as last night, which is a good sign.

"You know what, you look cute being all riled up,"he remarks and my mouth hangs open because talking about my cuteness today is the last thing I expected.

"Now tell me, why are we here and what happened to you yesterday?"

"Okay, but let's get outside," he effortlessly takes my hand in his and leads me outside to a little park area. He starts narrating the incident.

"Last evening, when I was driving to your house, I realized a car was hurtling towards me from the opposite direction, with very high speed. My instinct kicked in, and I swerved to the right, desperately trying to avoid the collision. And the person in the other car did the same to avoid the accident.

My car shuddered but remained unharmed and came to a screeching halt by the roadside. I thought we both luckily avoided the collision, but my breath caught in my throat when I heard a loud thud seconds after. As I glanced back at the wreckage behind me, the car had collided with a standing truck.

And the person was badly injured and unconscious. With the help of others, we took him to the hospital in the ambulance. Somehow they were able to contact his parents."

He was narrating the story normally, but      now at this point I can see the glisten in his eyes and the shakiness in his voice.

He continues "I see them crying hysterically for their son, who was laying there unconscious, covered in plaster and band-aids. That was so heart-wrenching to see"

"And... then... then..." it seems like he would choke on his own words.

"Hey, hey, it's okay," I say, giving a rub to his shoulders.

"And then his parents started blaming me for his condition. They said I am responsible for him being in the hospital; he was trying to save me."

"What bullshit! If he was trying to save you, you were doing the same. And also, you weren't on the wrong side, he was."

"I was so shaken. The doctor said they don't know when he will regain consciousness. I didn't know what to do; it was horrifying." Tears are streaming down his cheeks. I want to yell at those people who blamed him for the accident.

I cup his face and wipe his tears with my thumb. "Look at me." His eyes meet mine. "It was not your fault at all." He nods his head a little. Then I hug him; I can feel his body relaxing in my embrace.

"He is awake and doing good now," he says, breaking the long hug. "The hospital called me this morning to tell me he regained consciousness. That's why I left so early."

"You should call Raj Bhaiya; he tried calling you too. He must be concerned."

As he gets busy with the call, I make my way inside the hospital to meet those parents. I am angry that they blamed Ishan, but as I look at them with their son, all my anger disappears. They are smiling, with their son, but their condition clearly shows how hard last night was for them.

I meet them and offer to pay the bills, only to know that Ishan has already taken care of it. I also ask them to apologize to Ishan, which they do. I don't want him to live with the guilt that he was the reason behind someone's pain.

The parents tell me that they were not thinking straight and not in their right mind, so they put all the blame on Ishan, and they really didn't mean it.

"Wanna grab something to eat?" I ask as we both make our way outside the hospital.

"Yeah, sure, but first, I need a shower," he points at himself.

---

Now I am in his kitchen because I was thirsty while he is taking a shower.

I am about to go into the living room, but an idea strikes in my mind.

After around 20 minutes, he comes out. "Let's go—" he stops mid-sentence as he sees the scene in front of him.

He looks at the dining table, then at me, and again at the dining table. He is at a loss for words.

"Welcome," I say with a proud smile.

"But I didn't say thank you," his own face breaks into a genuine smile.

"But you should."

"Thank you," he eyes the food on the table with excitement. "I didn't know that you can cook."

"Yeah, I have a few hidden talents."

"I hope I don't have to say, 'keep this talent of yours hidden' after taking a bite."

"I hope not"

We both settle ourselves. "No, but genuinely, thank you," he says, serving himself. I did this to cheer him up because he is still a little down.

He takes the first bite. "Damn, whoever is going to marry you is surely lucky as hell."

"What do you mean? Are you calling yourself lucky?"

He chuckles. "Sure, sure."

After a moment of silence, I speak. "I am glad that everything is fine now. You were so miserable last night."

"Yeah, but there were compensations."

"What compensations?"

"You."

Okay, now let me choke on my orange juice.

~~~

Title: Chapter : 18
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

We are currently playing the first T20 match of the tri-series against Afghanistan at the scenic grounds of Dharamshala. Afghan team has set a target of 159 runs for us to chase.

As the Indian innings begins, Rohit bhaiya and I stride to the crease to open for India. The crowd roars with excitement as Rohit bhaiya stands on the striker's end.

The first ball is bowled, and Rohit bhaiya faces it with concentration. It's a dot ball, met with a sigh of disappointment from the fans. But the next ball sees action. Rohit bhaiya strikes it, hoping for a quick run. However, I remain rooted in my place, my eyes fixed on the ball. I signal Rohit bhaiya to stay put, but it's too late. Rohit bhaiya, already in motion, and now  we both are on the non-striker's end. He shouts at me to run, but it's all too late; the Afghan fielders have already done their job.

Rohit bhaiya gets out on a duck, his innings cut short before it even begins, and it's my fault.

The Afghan team erupts in celebration at their first wicket of the match.

Frustration and anger flicker across Rohit bhaiya's face as he leaves the field, but not before shooting accusing / murderous glances in my direction. And I just stand there looking at him with a guilty expression. Of course, it was my fault; I could have run and made it to the striker's end.

I tried to move on from that and focus on making as many runs as I can, but luck is definitely not on my side today. First, I caused the captain to lose his wicket, and now, in an attempt to play a risky shot which I could have avoided, I also got out, making just 23 runs in 12 balls.

With a defeated look, I settle myself in the very last chair of the dugout. I know I will hear an earful from the captain and coaches after the game.

I am sulking and brooding over my performance and the run-out when I hear someone calling my name. It's Axar bhaiya, emerging from the dressing room. "Tere phone pr Ishan ka call aa rha hain," he says, handing me my phone with Ishan's name flashing on it.

I swipe the green icon and say, "Hello."

"Shubman, it wasn't your fault."

"Huh? What?"

"I mean the run-out, it wasn't your fault."

"Oh, uh... but if only I had run, then Rohit bhaiya wouldn't have lost his wicket... and wait.... are you watching the match?"

"Of course, I am... and don't sulk over that. Whatever happened was just miscommunication between you and Rohit bhaiya." He says in a comforting manner.

"Yeah, but even after that, I myself didn't do any great and got out."

"No, you played a good little inning there. Aur vaise bhi, we don't have to chase 200."

"But what if we lose and the blame comes on me?"

He softened his tone even more,"Meri jaan..."Meri jaan, the first time he called me that, it caught me off guard, but later I realized he uses it for anyone, like literally for anyone (and that kind of irked me, but I am not going to acknowledge it.)

"........Firstly, we are not going to lose, and secondly, win or lose cannot be decided by a single player's performance. It's a team game, and you know that better than anyone."

"Yeah, but still..."

"I knew you were sulking over it; that's why I called. Stop overthinking, Gill. It's cricket; we have to move on from one ball to another." The fact that he knew I was in a bad mood, and the fact that he called me to comfort me, made me feel warm from inside.

"Did you check social media?" He ask randomly.

"No."

"Wait, I'm gonna send you something."

Ishan:

Ishan:

Ishan:

Me:Damn 😂😂

Ishan:And see this one

Me:Agree😂

I am sitting in the dugout, smiling and laughing at the memes that Ishan is sending me, even after making the captain sacrifice his wicket. And scoring very less runs myself, but who cares?

Who cares when your boyfriend-oops!-fake boyfriend is sending you memes to cheer you up?

I'm sure I'm going to get trolled for laughing (maybe even breathing right now).

Ishan's phone call and his message have put me into a much better mood.

I opened social media only to be overwhelmed by the sheer amount of trolling I'm receiving, which makes me realize that Ishan made an effort to search for those non-degrading and funny memes to send me. And I feel grateful to him.

Me:Ishan

Ishan:Haan?

Me:I am going to tell rohit bhaiya that youwere saying ki run out was his fault.

Ishan:GADDARI KORBE 😾

Me:😂😂

---

I got benched for the second and third match, which I saw coming, not gonna lie, but at least they didn't say I have back spasms, ankle injuries, or groin pain.

After the third T20 match wrapped up, which was damn interesting with two super overs in it, I called Ishan. He told me he missed the match, so I started explaining every interesting detail to him while he watched the match highlights.

"And you know when 2 runs were needed from 1 ball in the first super over, Rohit bhaiya retired out and sent Rinku, you know why?"

"So he can run fast?"

"Exactly," and we both laughed.

I continued telling him more about the super overs and the match overall, and he kept listening keenly.

"Someone was saying 'kya din aa gye hain aab afganistan ke saath do-do super over krne padd rhe hain', who is going to tell them that the Afghanistan team is becoming better and better."

After I finished narrating the whole two super over scenario, I started telling him about the celebration, how Virat bhaiya danced to the "Moye Moye" song, and that slide he did to reach where the other players were standing for a group photo.

He also kept giving his input in between to assure me that he is very much interested in whatever I am telling him.

After some time, the conversation slipped from cricket to other random topics, and we literally talked till 2 freaking AM.

---

"What's going on between you two?"

"I myself don't know."

"What do you mean?"

"He never says anything directly."

"Well, I don't know about the direct part, but he definitely starts acting nicer around you."

"Hawwww, are you jealous, Mr. Gill?"

"My ass."

"Yes, your ass is jealous."

"Shut up, Nia."

She just rolls her eyes.

"You deserve better."

"I know, right?" With a little pause, she continues, "but someone has to tolerate him and be the unlucky one."

"I see you are quite eager to be the 'unlucky' one," I say in a mocking tone.

She just flashes a toothy smile at me and hurries away as someone calls her.

I look at Darshan, who is busy with one of the staff discussing something, and then I look at Nia. They have worlds-apart personalities, but they'd look good together.

We are currently at a palace in Jaipur for a photoshoot, the photoshoot of an apparel brand featuring me and Ishan. I scan the whole place and find him standing with his manager discussing something serious.

Traitor! He just left me alone here to stand; I deserve better than this. #Justiceforme

He looks in my direction, and I wave at him. I have no shame. The shoot is going to start a bit late, and I'm just getting bored watching everyone running here and there doing their jobs.

He walks to me but not before getting stopped by three people who ask for his autograph and selfie. After a decade, when he finally comes to stand beside me, he ask, "What's up?"

"Everything is down, I am getting bored."

A person passing by hears this and says to us, "Well, you two can have a look around the place until the shoot starts."

"Yes, we should do that," Ishan says enthusiastically.

"Okay, let's go."
