Title: Chapter : 19
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

He walks to me but not before getting stopped by three people who ask for his autograph and selfie. After a decade, when he finally comes to stand beside me, he ask, "What's up?"

"Everything is down, I am getting bored."

A person passing by hears this and says to us, "Well, you two can have a look around the place until the shoot starts."

"Yes, we should do that," Ishan says enthusiastically.

"Okay, let's go."

Even though the palace is closed to the public today, we have managed to secure a tour guide.

He is a young boy, probably in his early twenties, guiding us through different parts of the palace.

He seems very chatty, and in no time, he and Ishan have clicked. Ishan can make friends with anyone, LITERALLY, even with a tree.

He shows us the weaponry and armor hall, which is really huge, and tells us about them, such as a specific weapon used by a particular king or which weapon killed which enemy.

Then he shows us a palanquin collection, providing details about the materials used in making them.

Ishan is asking him a lot of questions about the stuff that we are seeing, and the boy is more than happy to answer those questions, with a smile never leaving his face. Not just that, but his eyes never leave Ishan's face.

He doesn't even spare a single glance in my direction, as if I don't even exist at all.

It's not like I am dying to get his attention but I don't like the way he is looking at Ishan, ADMIRINGLY, A BIT TOO MUCH ADMIRINGLY.

We are in the main hall, and to make my presence known to that boy, I take Ishan's hand in mine and keep walking, pretending as if it's the most natural thing we do. Ishan is kind of taken aback by that but doesn't withdraw his hand, which is good; otherwise, imagine I would be making a complete fool out of myself in front of the tour guide boy.

I notice that the smile on the boy's face dims when he glances at our intertwined hands. Mission accomplished. Wohoo.

He takes us to the remaining part, this time focusing more on the things he is describing instead of eye fucking Ishan.

When we are done touring the whole palace, which took more time than we initially thought, that boy asks for a picture together.

"So you know us?" Ishan ask him with a hint of amusement.

"Of course, who doesn't?" the boy replies, taking out his phone. I take his picture with Ishan, and Ishan takes one with me and the boy.

I am sure he wanted a picture only with Ishan, but out of courtesy, he begrudgingly takes one with me.

"Have fun shooting here," he says cheerily, looking towards Ishan and giving him a hug before leaving.

I am surprised that he didn't kiss Ishan before leaving or proposed him with the royal palace as backdrop.Fucker.

"Ishan, you are so innocent," I say, looking at the retreating figure of that boy.

"That's so random. But why?"

"Didn't you see how that boy was looking at you?"

"Oh, so you're jealous that he was charmed by me? Huh? I know I am quite charismatic," his lips curl into a proud smile.

I look at him with a pitiful smile. "Did you forget to take your pills?"

"What kind of pills?"

"Anti-delusional pills!"

He rolls his eyes.

---

I see Ishan stepping out of the vanity van, wearing ethnic attire similar to mine, and oh boy, he looks absolutely gorgeous in that.

The photoshoot begins, and the first set of photos is taken with us sitting on the stairs.

Then, we use the royal gates as backdrops, which have intricate designs crafted on them.

We have limited areas for shooting, as most parts of the palace are off-limits for any kind of photography.

But the team does a great job. Ishan and I change outfits a total of six times.

When we see the pictures, I am quite impressed with how they turned out.

I post a few behind-the-scenes pictures on my Instagram, and fans go wild over them, showering us with all kinds of positive comments. From expressing how much they wanted us together in a photoshoot to telling us how absolutely stunning we look together in those attires. They are in for a treat. I can only imagine their reaction when they finally see the photoshoot pictures. I smile at the comments when Nia squeals in joy, "Listen, listen," she asks for my and Darshan's attention.

"There is a huge carnival going on not far from our hotel. I attended it a few years back. We should go there," she says with infectious excitement. And even though we are tired, we don't have the heart to say no to her.

So we find ourselves at the carnival festival—me, Ishan, Nia, and Darshan.

Nia explains everything to us with a lot of joy, and I notice Darshan listening to her not only keenly but... I don't know how to describe it, more like he wants to listen to her, and only her. It seems like even if she were saying some utter crap, Darshan would listen to her with the same admiration that he is showing now.

This Darshan seems strange. I am used to a grumpy Darshan. I never imagined him like this.

Then I look at Ishan; he is also listening to Nia with interest, but his gaze lacks the admiration.

Then I look at Darshan again and think, how would it feel to be in love? To be soft only for that person? To show a completely different side of yourself to the person you love... To look at them as if they are the center of your universe. OR to be the jam of their Jim-jam biscuit (okay may be not the last one). It all must be a different experience. An experience I hope to have someday in the future.

I look at Ishan again, and he looks beautiful with that natural smile adorning his face.

"Who is coming to the Ferris wheel with me?" Nia ask

No one responds.

"What is this? Don't be party poopers, guys," she says with a fake angry expression.

We agree to her, and Darshan is the last one to agree.

And in the Ferris wheel, we find out why he was the last one to agree. Because he is shit scared and the whole time he just screams his lungs out, holding Nia's hand while the three of us are laughing our asses off.

Thank God we agreed to come to the carnival; otherwise, I could have missed the opportunity to see a scared Darshan.

Now if Darshan says anything to me or shows me his anger, I will pull the 'at least I am not shit scared of the Ferris wheel' card or 'at least I don't scream my lungs out holding my assistant's hand on a Ferris wheel' card, that will shut him up.

I am seeing too many unknown versions of Darshan in a single day, which I seem to really enjoy.

After a while, we get separated into two groups, Ishan and me, Nia and Darshan.

We explore the carnival, did different activities, and ate delicious food. Even Ishan seems to enjoy it despite the tiredness from the photoshoot.

When it is time to return, I see Darshan and Nia, Darshan being barefoot, fixing his shoes on Nia's foot and Nia's heel tied to the sling bag which Darshan is holding.

"Oh, my eyes, my eyes," I say dramatically and keep a hand over my eyes.

"What are you doing, Gill?" A confused Ishan asks.

"Can't handle this much love, I'll get diabetes."

When darshan and Nia stride towards us, I throw a hand over Darshan's shoulder (something I have never done) like we are besties or something.

"You should propose to her," I give him a huge grin, and his face shows a hint of blush...and oh my god, I could tease him endlessly.

---

Ishan enters the room. He appears a little drunk but sober enough to walk on his own. He walks towards me with steady steps, slowly leaning over my lying figure and placing both of his hands on either side of me.

"What are you doing, Ishan?" I ask as my pulse quickens.

"Can I kiss you?" he asks with a needy tone in his voice, his gaze lingering on my lips.

"What... I mean why?" I stammer under his scrutiny.

~~~

Title: Chapter : 20
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

I sense someone's presence at the doorstep, and I struggle to fully open my eyes due to the heaviness in my eyelids.

But wait a minute, how could someone enter the room when both Ishan and I are in bed? Didn't we lock the door last night?

After blinking several times, I can see clearly that it's not just one person but several standing there: Virat bhaiya, Hardik bhaiya, Shreyas, and Surya Bhai.

They all wear stunned expressions as they look in our direction, and that's when it dawns on me that Ishan and I are in a cuddling position with my arm loosely wrapped around his neck, and his own hand placed over my chest.

Feeling a little embarrassed, I attempt to get up only to be stopped by Ishan's sleepy figure who snuggles more into me. I glance back at our spectators, and try to form a coherent sentence, but my drowsy brain fails me.

Hardik bhaiya speaks up, "Our practice was supposed to start at 7:30." My gaze snaps towards the clock, and it's already 8:05. Oh no.

Ishan and I checked into the hotel very late last night because our flight from Jaipur got delayed. Instead of going straight to bed, we decided to watch a horror movie and ended up scaring the shit out of ourselves. But fortunately, we were able to use the washroom. ALONE.

In conclusion, because of all that, we slept very, very late.

"I want you two on the team bus within 10 minutes," Virat bhaiya says sternly.

All of them leave the room, but Shreyas lingers a little longer, staring into my soul as I free myself from a clingy Ishan and get out of bed.

With an amused expression, Shreyas remarks, "Last time I checked, you two were fake boyfriends."

WHAT?

Before I can say anything, he leaves too, leaving me standing here dumbfounded.

I shift my focus towards Ishan, "Ishan... Ishan, wake up," I shake his shoulders in an attempt to wake him, but there's no response.

"Ishan, wake up, otherwise Virat bhaiya will eat us alive," I cry out, earning a response from him. In a low voice and sleepy tone, he says, "Virat bhaiya ain't a cannibal," He changes his position, "Let me sleep a little more."

"ISSHHAAANNNN, PLEASE," I literally shout over his head.

It takes me a few more minutes of struggle, but he finally gets out of bed.

And that struggle included tickling him, which worked.

---

We both enter the bus, and everyone's head snaps in our direction as we are the last ones to arrive. But Ishan could not care any less right now as he is still very groggy. We both settle at our destined seats and within minutes I find him fast asleep, my shoulder serving as his personal pillow. His face is relaxed and his breath gentle and rhythmic.

And this does not go unnoticed by the team members around. My thoughts wander back to the day we both started sleeping on the same bed. I found us cuddling each other the next morning, even though we both usually sleep on our respective sides of the bed. But this fact is not known to Ishan because I am always the one to wake up first.

I don't know who snuggles up to whom, but in the morning I find both of us in the middle of the bed, cuddling.

If Virat, Hardik, and Surya Bhai throw weird looks towards us, Ishan will just think that they are being weird.

I think it's quite normal to cuddle someone, I mean come on, we are teammates and even friends now, right? People need to be open-minded.

On the grounds, our first practice session of the day goes quite peacefully, and now everyone is just lying around, relaxing, chugging down water bottles, and pouring the remaining water on their faces to cool themselves off.

"You know, if my roommate tried to snuggle up to me while sleeping, I would rather change rooms," says Surya Bhai, throwing teasing glances towards me and Ishan.

"What are you talking about? Your roommate is Shreyas. Is he like that?" asks a confused Siraj Bhai.

"Nope, but some people are," Shreyas Bhai also chimes in the conversation.

Siraj Bhai looks at me for some explanation, but I just shrug, pretending I don't know what they are talking about. When I glance at Ishan, he is unbothered by the ongoing conversation, playing with a puppy who is sitting on his tummy. We didn't talk much today except when needed during our practices.

When the break is over, I stand up, removing the dust from my pants, and notice that Ishan is fast asleep. He can sleep anywhere, anytime.

"Wake up, you sleeping koala," I sprinkle water over his face. He opens one of his eyes and touches his cheeks. "Is it raining?"

I give him a hand, which he takes, and he gets up. "No, but if you keep sleeping here, you'll definitely see raining, raining of Rohit bhaiya's anger."

"I am angry at you," he says, picking up his gears.

"For what?"

"You didn't let me sleep 5 more minutes today," he walks a little ahead of me.

"You gotta be kidding me, I saved your ass from getting lectured by Virat and Rohit bhaiya."

"Whatever."

I pull him back a little, playfully gripping his arm.

"Oi, don't 'whatever' me." Now he is face to face with me, his big doe eyes boring into mine, and this situation triggers something in my brain.

Ishan enters the room; he seems a little drunk but sober enough to walk on his own. He walks towards me with steady steps, slowly leans over my lying figure, keeping both of his hands on either side of me.

"What are you doing, Ishan?" I ask as my pulse quickens.

"Can I kiss you?" He asks with a needy-ness  in his voice, his gaze lingering on my lips.

"What... I mean, why?" I stammer under his scrutiny.

"We are boyfriends, meri jaan," now our lips are dangerously close, his warm breath mingling with mine.

"But we are fa------mhhmmm..." He cut me off as he kisses the word 'fake' out of my mouth; the touch of his soft lips feels heavenly. I close my eyes, feeling every move of his lips.

He cups my face with one hand, deepening the kiss. The moment feels blissful.

He breaks the kiss and looks me in the eyes, searching for something. "Do you regret it?"

I answer his question by kissing him back.

The whole dream flashes in front of my eyes, and in a moment of horror, I push Ishan back. "What are you doing? And why do you look so pale as if you have seen a ghost?"

I dare to meet his gaze. What the heck was that dream? And why do I remember it so clearly? And why did it feel so real and why did it feel so--

"Shubman? Shubman? What happened?"

I can hear him very well, but my mind is very much occupied by the dream.

"GILL?" he waves his hand.I just stand there without moving or saying anything.

"Are you sick?" He asks, putting the back of his hand over my forehead. I flinch, taking a step back.

"Why are you behaving weird all of a sudden?" He waits for my answer, but nothing comes, so he continues, "Okay, I am not angry with you! Happy?"

Happy? No.Traumatized? Very much.

"Now stop behaving weird and let's get back to practice," he starts dragging me while holding my wrist. How will he react if he finds out about the unholy dream I had about him?

---

I busied myself in practice, pushing the image of that dream back in my mind. But it keeps flashing in front of me again and again. Even if I close my eyes for a few seconds, the image of that nonsense dream is there. Ugh, what is this shit?

After practice ended, Yuzi bhai asked us to join him to go for a film. Ishan declined, saying he is tired, but I saw this as a great opportunity to divert my mind and forget about the dream, so I agreed to go with him.

The film was great and it indeed helped me keep that dream away. But when we returned to the hotel and I stood in front of my room, my mood soured as I saw Ishan hugging someone tightly.

I don't know the person he is hugging.

Cousin? Friend? Boyfriend? Real one?

~~~

Title: Chapter : 21
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

When we return to the hotel and I stand in front of my room, my mood sours as I see Ishan hugging someone tightly. I don't know the person he is hugging. Cousin? Friend? Boyfriend? Real one?

Whatever their relationship might be, the hug they are sharing is not sitting well with me. I am just standing here, waiting for Ishan to acknowledge my presence, which he does when his big, joy-filled eyes shift away from that man and land on me.

"Oh, you are here!" He says, looking toward me as I enter the room, glancing between him and the unknown person.

"Meet Mayank," he gestures towards the unnamed man who now has a name.

With a smile, Mayank extends his hand in my direction for a handshake "Hi"

"Hello" I reciprocate with him.

"He is my childhood best friend," as soon as the words leave Ishan's mouth and register in my brain, I tighten my grip over Mayank's hand, applying a bit too much pressure in the handshake.

He flinches a bit and looks at our joined hands, and I instantly let go of his hand.

Ishan continues providing information about Mayank which I didn't ask for."He works in Bangalore."Then what is he doing here?"He is here for his office-related work." Do I care?

"And he is......." Ishan gestures towards me, but before he can complete his sentence, I cut him off, "I am his boyfriend," I blurt out and receive an astonished look from him.

"Yeah pretend boyfriend" Mayank's smile turning into a grin.

"Ummm Shubman, actually he knows," Ishan looks at me with a sheepish smile,"That we aren't boyfriends for real."

"Oh."Of course he knows, after all, he is Ishan's 'ChiLdHoOd bEsTfRiEnD', how can he not know?

"Actually, he was there with me when the news of our dating broke out; he was the first one who told me."

Oh, so they were together... doing what exactly? And now I remember Ishan's manager telling me about Ishan's short vacation trip when we were at the meeting.

So, Mayank, his 'ChiLdHoOd bEsTfRiEnD'was with him on the vacation. Nice.

I didn't realize that I am staring at Mayank a bit too intensely, so I avert my gaze.

"Mayank, why didn't you tell me that you were coming?" Ishan asks, completely turning his attention towards him.

"I wanted to give you a surprise, Sweetheart."

Sweetheart? No, no, SWEETHEART. He calls him sWeETheArT.I practically cringe at that word because who on earth uses that word, especially in the 21st century? It's a very cringe-worthy word indeed.

Don't tell me Ishan also uses some cringe-worthy word for his childhood best friend.

Fortunately, he doesn't.

___

In the evening, I find myself sitting with Ishan and Mayank in a nearby cafe. They are both catching up and jumping from one topic to another without any break, and I feel like I am third-wheeling them. I try to fit myself into their conversation, but it feels so forced that I shut up and let them have their talk.

Sipping my drink, I look at Mayank. He has average looks. Well, maybe not average, but certainly not better than mine. He is shorter than me, and I look better than him. Plus, I have dimples. Yeah.

WHY THE FUCK I AM COMPARING MYSELF TO HIM?

When it's time to return, hisbEsTiE, as known as Mayank, also comes with us because he left his bag in our room. As we enter the room, Ishan goes to pick up the bag to hand it to Mayank, but in the meanwhile, I accidentally push a flower vase, which is about to shatter on the floor. Before it can reach the ground, both Mayank and I attempt to catch it, but in doing so, a sharp edge of that vase injures both of us.

We both wince in pain as the sharp edge cuts our skin. Ishan comes running towards us and carefully holds the vase from the bottom, putting it aside. "What are you two doing?" he asks, looking towards our hands. "Oh god, there is blood."

I got a cut on my thumb and index finger, and Mayank got one on his palm.

Ishan rushes to get a first aid box. He makes both of us sit on the bed and cleans the blood from our hands. I can see Mayank's injury is bigger than mine.

It's not serious but definitely lengthy. Once the blood stops, Ishan starts putting some medicine over Mayank's cut.

Can't he see I am here too and I am injured too? ("Stop acting like a bitch, Shubman, you can put the medicine on your cut yourself, and you know Mayank's hand is more hurt than yours."-- my sane thoughts speaks)

Of course, he will tend to his childhood best friend. Who am I? A teammate? A friend? I am not a ChiLdHoOd bEsTfRiEnD' after all.

After putting the medicine over his hand, Ishan throws the tube in my direction. I SWEAR TO GOD, THE AUDACITY OF THIS MINION.

MATLAB BC USKO HURT HUA HAIN MUJHE NAHI ? USKO KHUD KE HAATH SE LAGAYA AUR MAI YAHA PAGAL BAITHA HOON

[He got hurt, but didn't I get hurt too? He's applying medicine to him, and am I just sitting here like a fool?]

After he is done with his best friend'ssevaa(service), he bids him goodbye with a hug, and I am sitting here with an open cut, with a frown on my face. When Ishan looks back at me and sees me sitting in the same position with the tube lying beside me, he asks"Why didn't you apply it?"

"You applied it to him and not me," I say without letting the disappointment in my voice show, but I guess my face shows it because his eyes soften. "Your cut is small, you can do it without help. His cut was big, that's why I did that"

"Of course," I say curtly but still not moving or doing anything. I just stare at my cut, there is redness around it. I know I shouldn't get hurt over this, but I am hurt over this.

I know I am acting like a bitch, but he is also acting like one. So it's a one-one

He stands in front of me and then lowers his head to my level as I am sitting on the bed, his warm, big, dark brown orbs looking into mine. (Those beautiful eyes.)

He flicks my head. "Ooowww." I rub my forehead with my non-injured hand.

"You are such a drama queen, Gill," he then comes to sit beside me, takes the medicine tube, takes the injured hand in his, and starts applying the medicine. "How are you going to survive alone?" ("Why? aren't you going to be there? always?" - a voice speaks from somewhere deep inside, but I ignore it.)

"No, no. The question is how you survived all these years." He puts a bandage over it. I just keep staring at him.

And then he presses it lightly. "Aaahhh."

I look at him, and with a smile, he says, "This is for acting like a baby, a giant baby."

It's generous of him to call mebabyand not abitchbecause that's how I am behaving right now.

___

"Come on, focus on the ball," Rohit bhaiya shouts as we practice fielding.

"Tu udhar apne boyfriend ki taraf kya dekh raha hain Kishan? Focus on the ball," shouts Virat bhaiya and I glance towards Ishan, but he looks away.

["Why are you looking over there at your boyfriend, Kishan? Focus on the ball."]

"Aab tu apne boyfriend ko kyu dekh raha hain Gill?" Hardik bhaiya points out "Focus on the practice," Jaddu bhaiya adds, asserting his seniority.

["Why are you looking at your boyfriend now, Gill?"]

After the fielding practice, I start batting in the nets, with Siraj Bhai bowling to me.

A little into the practice and "Aaaaahhhh!" I wince in pain as the ball hits me just above my ankle.

"Shit, sorry, sorry," Siraj Bhai rushes towards me, and everyone else's heads snap in my direction. Ishan doesn't take much time to reach me. "Kya ho gya? [What happened ] Why did you shout?" he asks with panic.

"Ball uske pair pr lag gayi," Siraj Bhai indicates where the ball hit as I sit here holding my leg.["The ball hit his leg."]

Soon, everyone else gathers around me.

"Tune woh kyu nhi pehna tha" Rohit bhaiya examines the injury.["Why didn't you wear that?"]

"Pads....pads kyu nhi pehna hua tha?"Virat bhaiya clarifies Rohit bhaiya's 'woh'. ('that')

["Pads...Why didn't you wear pads?]

Ishan hits me on the head and says "Isko bohot hero banna hain."["He wants to act like a hero"]

I look at him with hurtful puppy eyes. "I'm already hurt, and now you're scolding me."

"Who said to practice without wearing pads?" Virat bhaiya extends his hand. I take it and get up.

"I told him to wear them, but he didn't listen," Siraj bhaiya replies.

In the dressing room, the physio applies an ice pack to the injury, which has now turned purple, to soothe it. He also gives me an ointment to apply. Ishan quickly takes the ointment and puts it in his pocket.

"Jyada dard ho raha hain?" ishan asks as everyone else left to continue the practice.["Is the pain too much?"]

"Abhi toh nhi ho raha itna , baad me ho sayad"["It's not hurting that much right now, maybe later it will."]

"Kuch chaiye?"["You want anything?"]

"Paani"["Water"]

He retrieves a water bottle, swiftly unscrews the cap, and instead of passing it to me, he gently guides the bottle into my mouth, allowing me to drink from his hand. I show no resistance and begin to drink.

"Arey bhai karwachauth hain kya aaj?" Surya Bhai scoff teasingly leaning on the door. Ishan just rolls his eyes.

["Hey, is it Karwa Chauth today?"]

____

Currently, I sit upright on the bed, leaning comfortably against the headboard with my legs extended before me. Ishan occupies the lower end of the bed, tenderly applying ointment to the bruised area on my leg.

"Only yesterday, you cut your fingers, and today you got hit by the ball."

"What's your point?"

"You're clumsy, Gill."

"Ohhh... but it's okay to be clumsy when you have a boyfriend to take care of you!" I say teasingly.

Ishan pauses his hand and looks towards me with a serious expression.

Did I just offend him?

"I'm just kidding, Ishan. I didn't mean to--ummm..." My heartbeat quickens as I panic to find the words.

He stares at me without saying anything or changing his expression. Oh god, I messed up.

He stands up, but I grab his wrist and glance back at him. "Listen to me... oh god... please don't go. I was just joking with you."

He breaks into fits of laughter. I'm confused for a second, then realize he was messing with me.

"Ishaaaaaaannnnnn," I whine, but relieved to see him laughing.

"You should have seen your face, you looked so funny," he says between his laughs.

"It wasn't funny. Damn, I thought I offended you badly."

"Chill, Gill... see what I did there, 'chill Gill'?" He says, pulling my cheeks, and I protest.

He goes to take a shower, and when he comes out, he's wearing a white T-shirt, black jeans, and layering a black denim jacket over his T-shirt.

"Are we going somewhere?" I ask excitedly.

"Nope, not you, but I am going out........with Mayank."

My smile and heart both drop.

~~~

I don't know much about cricket injuries, I just wrote it aise hi so don't take it seriously!

THIS COMMENT CRACKED ME UPAnd the "🎀" 😭.

Title: Chapter : 22
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

He goes to take a shower, and when he comes out, he's wearing a white T-shirt, black jeans, and layering a black denim jacket over his T-shirt.

"Are we going somewhere?" I ask excitedly.

"Nope, not you, but I am going out........with Mayank."

My smile and heart both drop.

"Oh," is all I manage to say.

"Yeah, and you should rest. Your injury might be hurting."

Arrey, seedha seedha bolo na, saath nahi le jana. Aur sirf Mayank ke saath jana hai bahar.... 'YoUr inJurY miGht bEhUrtinNg'kya hota hai?

[Don't beat around the bush and just say it straight that you don't want to take me with you. And only want to go out with Mayank... What is ''YoUr inJurY miGht bEhUrtinNg']

"But..." I try to say that I am fine and can go with him, but I am not going to force myself on him.

Before leaving, he looks toward me. "You should get your food here only. All the bhaiyas (bros) have gone out with their families, so they won't be here for dinner."

Abbey ja na, apne sweetie patootie  adarak lassan ke paas. [Just go to your sweetie patootie, ginger garlic]"Okay"

And he left. Just after he left, I start thinking whether I should have stopped him or not.

My sane thought speaks -"Why should you have stopped him? He is just going out with his friend. What's wrong with that?"

My not-so-sane thought interjects - "He just left me here alone, that too when I have an injury, and also everyone else is out."

Sane thought - "Firstly, your injury isn't that big or serious. Secondly, he tended to it before leaving. Also, why do you (me) start acting like a bitch when Ishan gives even a little attention to someone else?"

Not-so-sane thought - "Shut up."

It hasn't been long since he left, and I'm already finding excuses to call him or ask him to return.

Should I say I have a high fever? (Sane thoughts -And what will you do when he will check your temperature? Put onions under your arms?)

Or should I say my leg is hurting badly? (Sane thought returns -Who hammered your leg out of nowhere? You were all right before he left.)

Or should I say I slipped in the bathroom? (Sane thoughts here -Firstly, that's a very lame excuse. And secondly, how would you have reached your phone to call him when you are on the bathroom floor screaming out of pain?)

I decide not to do anything and let him enjoy his time. Yeah, that's how it should be. I can survive on my own. Yeah.

"Hello, Ishan."

Barely 15 minutes later, I called him with a good excuse in my mind, and that excuse isn't even an excuse; it's reality.

"Ishan, what I am saying is that you are out with Mayank. What if the press took pictures and published an article stating that we broke up and now you are dating someone else, i.e., Mayank?"

He chuckles. "What are you saying, Gill?"

"No, but think about it. If that happens, there will be negative PR, and then we'll have to put on some stunt to counter those rumors." I say this with all the seriousness I can gather, but all I receive is another chuckle. BITCH.

"You're thinking too much, Gill. People know Mayank is my best friend. I have a lot of pictures with him on my Insta, so there won't be such rumors. Chill."

Now I am more pissed as I scroll through his Insta and see his picture with Mayank.

*Knock knock*

Siraj Bhai enters the room and sits on the other side of the bed. "How's that?" he asks, pointing toward the injury. "It's fine, it's not hurting that much."

"You have made me feel guilty."

"Don't be. It's my fault that I didn't wear the pads when you asked me to."

"Vaise, why are you sulking? And where is Kishan?"

"He is out with one of his friends," I reply, to which he retorts, "Makes sense!?"

"What makes sense?" I ask, confusedly.

"You being all dull and sulky."

"Huh?"

"I mean, Ishan and you."

"Ishan and I?"

"Umm, don't get me wrong, but sometimes it looks like the relationship you have with him is not just of a teammate or friend."

"Yeah, we are boyfriends," I try to joke, knowing well where this conversation is going.

"Yeah, that... To me, it doesn't look like you two are pretend boyfriends, especially from your side."

I just stare at him, urging him to continue. Whatever he is saying, we never talk this seriously. We always add some humor and sarcasm to the conversation, but right now, whatever he is saying, he is saying with all seriousness, and that's scaring me.

He continues, "The way you look at him and the way you get very gloomy when he is not around you. I mean, don't get me wrong here, maybe it's just me, but I think you have fee--"

"Stop, please stop," I cut him off. My heartbeat increases at his words. I know what he is trying to say, but that's not true. He should not say this. "Please don't say anything further," I plead. I know he is not teasing me here, and I hate it.

He stops and looks at me in the eyes, with a hint of pity in his eyes.

No, no, nope. He is just assuming things. Whatever he is saying or was going to say is not true. (Mf Sane thought from somewhere deep inside my mind:If he is wrong, then why didn't you want to hear what he was going to say?)

"SHUT THE FUCK UP, YOU LIL BITCH!"

Siraj Bhai's face looks confused and offended at the same time, and that's when I realises that I fucked up because I said that aloud.

"Oh my god, no... It wasn't..." I panic, looking at his face that looks so disappointed in me. "No, Siraj Bhai, trust me, it wasn't for you." He didn't say anything. I grab his hand. "Oh my god, please trust me. I was saying that to the little voice in my head. It wasn't for you. Please don't be upset with me."

I might break down right here right now. Firstly, I am physically hurt (emotionally too). Secondly, Ishan left me alone. Thirdly, I haven't had food. Now I say something to Siraj Bhai, which I never imagined saying to him, even in my dreams.

I am very close to crying, and I must be looking very pathetic because Siraj Bhai's expression softens.

"I am sorry. It really wasn't for you. I would never say something like that to you. Please trust me."

I think he is trusting me. He gives me a warm, brotherly hug. "Why are you so silly?" He chuckles a little.

(Sane thought:See, even Siraj Bhai knows that you are silly.)

"Shu----" I stop.

Siraj Bhai breaks the hug. "Is that little voice saying something stupid again?" he asks with a smile. I smile back and nods vigorously .

He asks me for dinner, but I deny him. After making sure that I am good, he leaves the room.

His words linger in my brain, but I decide to focus on a more important thing, and that is, --Why hasn't Ishan returned yet? It's already past 11.

I start pacing in the room, waiting for him. First, I thought to call him, but I decide to go against it; otherwise, Ishan will think I am just disturbing him because I have nothing better to do.

When I get tired because of all the pacing, I sit on the couch.

---

Ishan is hovering over me, and his eyes are looking directly into mine.

Why all this eerily looks like that dream I had the other day, the dream where Ishan kissed me. Am I in a dream again? Will he again kiss me? Will I let him kiss me? As I was contemplating all these thoughts, Ishan jerked back and stood there looking at me.

This ain't a dream. I slept on the couch waiting for him, and now he has returned.

"Uh, umm, I was waking you, but you pulled my hand in your sleep so--" he explains why he was hovering over me.

"Hmmm," I say in a half-sleepy stance. As I sit up on the couch, my stomach growls.

Ishan gives me a questioning look. "You haven't eaten anything yet?"

"No, I wasn't hungry," I lie. I was very much hungry, but my brain was busy thinking about lame ideas.

My stomach growls again.

"I think your stomach is definitely hungry right now."

I should be pissed at him right now, but I don't have the energy to.

"Let's go then; the restaurant might be about to close."He practically drags me to the restaurant area in my shorts and disheveled hair. "Wait, let me at least brush my hair."

"It's almost 12; no one is going to spare a glance at you."

"That's offensive."

The restaurant staff gives us weird looks. Of course, it's their closing time, and out of nowhere, two boys (one in shorts and sleepyhead) arrive to delay their closing time.

Ishan orders for me only because, of course, he had eaten with his childhood bestie Mayank. As I start eating, he starts doing something on his phone, probably posting a picture with Mayank, what else, but then he puts it down pretty quick.

"How was your da------ I mean, did you enjoy today?"

"Yeah.... It was long since we have gone for an outing, if we exclude our little vacation, which I had to cut short and ran back to Delhi."

"Because of the article," I say sharply. And in reply, he just nods.

"And it was because of me," he can hear the self-blame in my tone. "Everything went wrong because of me... if I hadn't asked you to help me... to be my pretend boyfriend..." I am going into self-blame.

I have to stop eating, and he must have seen something in my expression. "Hey, hey... why are you saying all this?" he asks in a soft tone and helps one of my hands with both of his. "Don't blame yourself for anything," he continues. "I myself agreed to help you; you didn't force me or anything," he rubs the back of my hand soothingly.

"And whatever happens after that wasn't anyone's fault. I already told you that I don't blame you for it."

I might cry. Why is he so nice?

Then he ruffles my already unkempt hair with affection, and I can feel a layer of warmth spreading over my chest. I might just melt here.

"And also because of all that, we both become such good friends."

Friends. Yeah. FRIENDS.

~~~

Title: Chapter : 23
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

He ruffles my already unkempt hair with affection, and I can feel a layer of warmth spreading over my chest. I might just melt here.

"And also because of all that, we both become such good friends."

Friends. Yeah. FRIENDS.

"Yeah"

I resume eating, and he gets busy with his phone again. A few minutes later, my phone pings with a notification: Ishan has just posted a selfie of himself with Mayank in which Ishan is holding the phone, and Mayank's arm is around Ishan's neck.

I look up from my phone and find Ishan looking at me. He knows I just opened the post he shared, so I feel obliged to like it unwillingly. "Nice selfie," I say, giving him a tight-lipped smile.

When he puts his phone down, I quickly unlike the post. In Rohit bhaiya's language,mera *woh* kare like, jis post mein Ishan uske so-called cHiLdhOoDbEsTeIke saath ho.

We both return to our room. "Did you pack your stuff?" he asks, stopping before the washroom.

"No, the flight is on Monday."

"Check the message in the group; the flight is tomorrow" he says, entering the washroom.

I open the group message, and there it is, the flight to England is tomorrow evening, which was supposed to be on Monday because flights are the busiest after tomorrow, so there were chances of our flight getting cancelled. We have an ODI tri-series with England.

Ishan comes out wearing a loose t-shirt and shorts.

"I was thinking of going home before the England tour, but now it's not possible," he says, removing his wristwatch and putting it on the table.

"Same."

His focus shifts to the bruise on my leg."Has it gotten any better?"

"Hmmm, it didn't hurt until I applied too much pressure on my leg."

"Come here, I will apply some more ointment; it seems to work well."

"Uh ummm, I will apply it myself," I say for the sake of saying it, but either he didn't hear it or pretends that he didn't hear it.

Now, once again, we are in the same position, with me leaning against the headboard with my legs extended and him sitting near my feet applying the medicine over the bruise.

"You know, I've got some inside news," his tone is happy and excited.

"What news?" I ask curiously, taking interest in whatever he has to say.

He crosses his legs and sits comfortably on the bed. "Because we are going there earlier, for the first 3 days we are staying at a different location and will shift on the 4th day because that's when the practice starts."

"Ohhhhhh."

"Yeah, and you know what's more interesting?"

I raise one of my eyebrows, encouraging him to continue. "I heard that it's a beautiful place and we will be allowed to explore it," his voice contains a lot of excitement.

"That's great," I smile.

"Also, it wouldn't be mandatory to go in a group; one can go anywhere they want alone."

"Ouuu so you are planning to hang out there alone?"

"No no, of course you'll be there with me, how can I leave that whiny ass of yours alone," he remarks sarcastically.

"Hey, I don't whine," I say, pretending to be offended. (What is real or un-pretend in your life? Huh?)

"Yes, you don't whine, you actually whine like a 5-year-old kid."

I throw a pillow in his direction, which he catches. He then throws the pillow back at me with double force, hitting me directly in the face. He starts laughing, and for some reason, his laugh sounds melodious to me.

We finally lie on the bed. There is some distance between us, like how we usually sleep every day. He is excitedly telling me about the place we are going to explore. After a while, he puts the phone on the side table and covers both of us with the blanket. My hand accidentally touches one of his under the covers, but he doesn't move his hand, and neither do I. And I know for a fact that he is fully awake right now.

I always find myself cuddling with him in the morning, but right now, this mere touch of hands seems more intimate than anything.

Our hands remain there. I should remove my hand, but I don't want to. It's like touching him makes me feel some kind of reassurance.

It's weird. It's comforting.

---

I open my eyes only to find Ishan looking at me, caught under my grip. I am holding him closely, my hand wrapped around him protectively.

I realize that this is the first time he's seen us like this because all the other times I got up first so he wasn't aware of the cuddling thing but now he does. He might think it's weird or feel awkward. I'm thinking all this when he says, "We should get up; we have packing to do." His tone doesn't suggest any annoyance or awkwardness.

"Uh huh," I say, removing my hand and getting up immediately.

After returning from breakfast, we both start packing our stuff.

I speak awkwardly, "About this morning, I, uh..."

He looks at me confusedly, "Yeah?"

"I mean, how we were, ummm..."

He realizes what I'm talking about, "Oh, don't act like we don't end up like that every morning."

"What?" My eyes widen, almost coming out of their sockets. What he just said make me shik Shak Shock. "...how do you know? I mean, I always wake up before you, so how..."

"Everyday it goes like this: I woke up before you, then found myself in your strong grip. I tried to get out of your grip, I failed, and then I slept again a little more."

I stare at him flabbergasted, my jaw almost touching the ground. I try to say something, but he speaks again, "And this is how I give myself excuses for a little extra sleep."

"Well, thank you for the explanation. My jaw can return to un-flabbergasted position now," I remark, coming out of the shock.

He laughs and says, "Don't act like that. We both know that you are a cuddly person."

"Oii, how can you say this? You also hug me in your sleep."

"Don't make up stuff, Gill."

"Hawww, I'm not making it up. Okay, now I'll record whenever you'll snuggle up to me."

"Okay, meri jaan, whatever helps you sleep at night," he says with a little scoff, then busies himself in packing.

After a long flight and a 2-hour bus journey, we checked into our hotel, and, of course, my room is paired with Ishan's because we are boyfriends (fake boyfriends).

We decide to go out in the evening as we are really tired from the journey.

---

"But first, let's eat somewhere. I am really hungry." We are going to a place which Ishan found online, but before that, we are going to fill our stomachs.

On our way, we find a really aesthetic outdoor cafe, and we decide to eat there. As we both were deciding what to order, a girl who was busy on her phone, looking quite lost, sits in the chair beside me. It seems like she isn't aware of her surroundings, and she doesn't know that she just sat beside a stranger. She looks quite distressed, and Ishan and I both look towards each other, not knowing what to say because we don't want to sound rude.

So, we wait for her to realize it herself. When she does, it takes her a moment to realize that she is sitting with complete strangers. "Oh my god, I am so sorry... I didn't... I didn't realize that-"

(Chapter 23: Gill's boyfriend for a reason)

Title: Chapter : 24
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

"But first, let's eat somewhere. I am really hungry." We are going to a place which Ishan found online, but before that, we are going to fill our tummy.

On our way, we find a really aesthetic outdoor cafe, and we decide to eat there. As we both were deciding what to order, a girl who was busy on her phone, looking quite lost, sits in the chair beside me. It seems like she isn't aware of her surroundings, and she doesn't know that she just sat beside a stranger. She looks quite distressed, and Ishan and I both look towards each other, not knowing what to say because we don't want to sound rude.

So, we wait for her to realize it herself. When she does, it takes her a moment to realize that she is sitting with complete strangers. "Oh my god, I am so sorry... I didn't... I didn't realize that...." she says in a thick English accent.

She starts to get up, and as I look around, I notice that all the other tables are occupied, so I stop her, saying, "It's okay. If you want to, you can sit here; the other tables are occupied anyway."

She gives a quick look around and then awkwardly sits back in her chair. She types something on her phone and sighs, her body language indicating that she is tense about something.

Ishan notices as well, "I shouldn't ask, but are you okay? You seem quite worried about something."

She glances towards Ishan, perhaps contemplating whether she should tell us or not.

After a long silent moment, she buries her face in her palms, sighs again, and in a low voice, she says, "I am getting married tomorrow. My parents arranged the marriage without my consent."

"So you don't want to get married?"

"Actually, I have a boyfriend, but he doesn't live here. I tried telling my parents about him, but they think I am just making up stuff to avoid getting married."

"Why don't you just run away?" I try being practical but failed.

The girl gives me a look which basically translates into:are you stupid or something?

"I can't do that to my parents. I just can't run away like that." She says again burying her face in her palms

"But what are you going to do then? They'll marry you off to someone else tomorrow," Ishan inquires softly.

She looks down dejectedly and whispers, "I don't know."

"Why don't you ask your boyfriend to show up before your parents? Then they'll believe that your boyfriend actually exists and you are not just making it up," I suggest, providing the obvious solution.

She grimaces, "He was supposed to come today, but his flight got cancelled. Now he is coming tomorrow, and when his flight lands, I would have been married by then."

Ishan and I both look at each other, not knowing what to say.

She puts her head on the table and starts hitting it lightly in frustration. Suddenly, she sits straight and moves her head to her left to look at Ishan and then to her right to look at me. I can see the light bulb going off in her head as her eyes gleam. "I have an idea," she says, getting excited, "but I need your help."

"What kind of help?" Ishan questions.

"I will tell, but please tell me you guys are going to help me," she pleads with her big blue eyes.

We assure her that we will do whatever we can in order to help her, and she proceeds to voice her idea to us. "Okay, so as I told you, my boyfriend is going to land around 4 pm, and the event is supposed to happen before 4 pm. So, if someone manages to distract everyone until my boyfriend arrives there, then I won't have to marry then."

"And how are we supposed to help in this?" asks Ishan.

"By distracting everyone," she replies.

We both look at each other, confused.

"How?" We both speak at the same time.

"Ummm," she looks above, trying to think of something, and then says, "yeah... what if one of you comes there at the time of marriage and pretends as if you were dating me and I am cheating on you by marrying someone else... like creat drama there." She pauses for a second and then continues, "Or better, one of you comes in groom's attire and claims that I promised to marry you... the drama will definitely delay the ceremony, and by that time, he'll arrive, and after that, I'll tell my parents everything."

"But you can ask a friend of yours to help you with this," I tell her.

"I can't. My parents know my friends, and if any one of them is going to do it, my parents will know that it's just an act."

She clasps both her hands and asks again, "So, are you guys going to help me?" She is looking at us with lots of expectations in her eyes.

Ishan glances towards me, and we both give each other a quick nod.

"Is there any chance that your family or friends being interested in cricket?" asks Ishan, just to confirm, because what if someone recognizes us there? It will be a big trouble for us.

She is a little baffled by the randomness of Ishan's question but replies anyway, "No, why?"

"Um, nothing. We will help you." As soon as the words leave my mouth, relief washes over her face, and she visibly relaxes in her chair.

"I should tell all this to my boyfriend. Wait a minute," she says, excusing herself to make the call.

"Are we doing the right thing?" I ask Ishan, feeling very skeptical about the whole situation.

"I think so. We assured her to help her, and also, no one is going to recognize us there," he says, then relaxes back in the chair and with a confident expression says, "Also, you can't expect epic shit from basic people." I chuckle looking at him, but inside I am not so confident about all this, but I don't have any choice now.

She returns after a while and announces, "Let's go."

"Where?" I ask.

"To buy the groom's attire," she says excitedly.

I look between her and Ishan. "Do we really have to go all out?"

"Yes, if we are doing this, let's do this properly," she says, squealing a little as if she is way too happy to execute her idea with full blown preparation.

After half an hour, we found ourselves in a showroom with Ellisa. It's her name that we got to know on our ride to the showroom.

After lots of debate, it's been decided that I'll be the main character in the whole act, which means I'll be the one making the dramatic entry into the wedding hall.

Ellisa handed me a black wedding tuxedo to try on, and when I draw the curtain, revealing myself in that tuxedo, I see Ishan sitting on the couch. When he looks up, his eyes scan me from head to toe, and then his gaze fixes on my face. I see something in his eyes, a shine, as if he is in some kind of trance. Even though his expressions are stoic, his eyes are saying something else.

"You are looking perfect," Ellisa exclaimed, which breaks Ishan's trance.

I give Ishan a pointed look to ask how I'm looking, and instead of saying anything, he just nods.Did I just leave you speechless with my charming looks, huh?

After purchasing the attire and shoes, as we are leaving the showroom, I see a black satin button-down shirt on a mannequin. I pull the fabric of Ishan's t-shirt to stop him as he was a little ahead of me and gesture towards the mannequin.

"You want to buy it?" he asks me.

"Yes, for you."

"No."

"Yes."

"Nooo."

"Why?"

"I already have lots of shirts."

"But look at this, it will look good on you."

"I am not going to buy it... let's go."

He goes out of the showroom, but I end up purchasing the shirt, FOR HIM.

---

As we enter the hotel, Surya Bhai, Shreyas, and Siraj Bhai have just returned from somewhere, and when they see the clothing bags, they ask, "You guys went shopping?"

First, we decided not to tell them anything, but we end up telling them everything.

"I want to see the drama unfold before my eyes," says Surya Bhai with the excitement of a 5-year-old boy who just got chocolates.

"Me too," chimes Shreyas.

"Same," Siraj Bhaialso joins.

"Nooooo, you can't come." Ishan exclaims

"What if you mess up something, so we will be there to help, right?" Reasons Surya Bhai.

"But you can't just crash someone's wedding." I interject.

"Then ask this girl to invite us," Surya Bhai says proudly giving the unasked solution.Ishan shakes his head. "Such national-level cricketers literally want an invitation to weddings so they can see drama. Tch tch tch."

"More like help in the drama?" says Shreyas with a toothy grin, being all excited.

With some more debate, it's been decided that they'll come along with me and Ishan.

To help? Nah.To enjoy the drama? Yes.

---

We make the most of our remaining time today by exploring a few places, and the experience was pleasant. Now, I am sitting on the bed using my phone while Ishan is taking a shower. Our room is dimly lit, with only the night bulb on.

Suddenly, I hear something: a creepy sound followed by the sound of something shattering. It's coming from the balcony of our room. Our room has an attached balcony with glass sliders that are slightly ajar, allowing a gust of wind to enter the room and make the curtains flutter before I can make sense of the situation.

I hear it again-a low, creepy hum that lasts merely for five seconds.

"What the heck!" I shout, quickly getting under the covers to protect myself from whatever is trying to scare me. (A small voice in my head chimes in: Seriously? Getting under the covers for protection? Isn't that what kids do? Who let you grow up?)

Hearing my shout, Ishan peeks out from the bathroom. "Why did you shout?"

"I heard something-something creepy. It probably came from the balcony," I say, peeking out from under the covers.

"Wait a minute," he says and closes the door again. The curtains on the sliders flutter again in the breeze, casting eerie shadows across the room. I panic. "Ishaaan!" I shout his name, my heartbeat going haywire.

I catch a glimpse of the outside view through the gap in the curtains, and what looked beautiful just a few hours ago now seems creepy to me.

"Ishan, you've got to come here! There's something on the balcony!"

A minute later, he emerges from the bathroom, towel draped over his shoulder and a mischievous grin on his face. "What's got you spooked? You've been watching too many horror movies."

"No, seriously, I heard something... It could be a ghost or..." I say sheepishly.

Ishan rolls his eyes playfully. "Ghosts don't exist."

"But I definitely heard something-a really creepy sound."

"Okay, let me check then." Before he can go any further, I stretch out my hand and hold his. "No, don't go there."

"Come on, at least let me check," he insists.

I tighten my hold. "No, no, no."

"Okay, let me close the sliders properly, otherwise it will spook you again," he says with a chuckle, closing the sliders and drawing the curtains properly to block the outside view.

After drying his hair, he settles himself on the bed while I keep looking towards the balcony, fearing another creepy sound. Ishan notices this and says, "You're getting creeped out by probably nothing."

I look at him and without saying anything, slide closer to him, still leaving some gap between us. Sensing my intention, Ishan spreads his arm, and without wasting a second, I put my head over his arm, and he wraps his hand around me protectively, giving me the excuse to snuggle more into him, wrapping my hand over his chest.

For a moment, I forget about my fear, feeling only a warm, fuzzy sensation spreading inside me-the comfort I feel under his embrace.

"Sleep," he says, gently placing his palm over my eyes, thinking I might be looking towards the balcony again. But who's going to tell him? I'm looking at his face. His beautiful face which is looking very kissab-- SHUT UP.

And just like that, we both sleep nestled together, like two peas in a pod.

---

The next morning

"Ishan, I'm really scared now."

"Of ghosts?"

"No... I'm scared that we'll end up messing up the whole 'shaadi rukwao' thing."

"What could possibly go wrong?"

(A LOT, APPARENTLY. YOUR FEELINGS CAN GET MESSED UP.)

~~~
