Title: Chapter : 25
Chapter_Text:
A quick recap: Ishan and Shubman both agree to help Ellisa delay her wedding by creating some drama!

---

•SHUBMAN GILL•

The room feels smaller with each step I take, the gravity of the situation drawing upon me. Yesterday, I approached the whole situation with nonchalance, not considering any consequences. But today, the weight of our plan presses down upon me, and I pace the room, feeling anxious.

On the other hand, Ishan looks quite calm, or at least he seems so, his demeanor not betraying any hint of the anxiety that I am feeling right now.

"Ishan," I begin, my voice betraying the tremor of uncertainty that is gripping me, "I'm actually scared."

He casts a sidelong glance in my direction and arches an eyebrow, asking, "Of ghosts?" he says, referencing last night's incident, a wry smile playing on his lips.

But I don't have time to get embarrassed over that. "No... I'm scared that we'll end up messing up the whole 'shaadi rukwao' thing."

A chuckle escapes Ishan's lips, though it lacks any real mirth. "What could possibly go wrong?" his words laced with confidence.

"I don't know, may be everything"

As the time is approaching, a weird sensation swirls inside me, probably anxiety, making me feel uneasy.

Ishan's movements disrupt the heavy silence of the room as he retrieves the wedding tuxedo from its resting place within the cupboard. As I take it, its weight feels heavy in my hand, It feels ominous.

I try to tie the bow but struggle with it. Ishan watches, looking as if he's waiting for me to call him for help. After seeing my struggle, he himself approaches towards me and takes matters into his own hands.

With precise movements, he starts to tie the bow. In the process, his cold fingers brush against my warm skin, sending a shiver down my spine and eliciting a physical response from me. Ishan either doesn't notice or pretends not to.

He ties the bow with perfection."Done," I turn around to look in the mirror, and the bow is perfect. "Where did you learn it?"

"YouTube tutorials."

"For me?"

"For who else?"

Okay, this one is smooth.

He helps me put on the jacket, and after doing so, he runs his hand over the lapels, a soft smile tugging at his lips. He takes a few steps back to look at me properly and  with a smile playing on his lips, "Not bad," he remarks casually.

"Just 'not bad'?" I counter. "You were practically speechless yesterday when you laid eyes on me in this." I tease him about his earlier speechlessness.

He playfully rolls his eyes, "Don't flatter yourself," he retorts with a grin. "I'm not going to say that you're looking utterly charming and have the capability to pull anyone you want right now."I don't think it was his intention, but somehow his words come out genuine.

Taking a deliberate step closer to him, I can't help but challenge his denial. "Hmm, did you realize you just praised me?"

A feigned look of innocence crosses his features as he takes a subtle step back. "No, I didn't," he insists, though I can see a flicker of amusement in his eyes.

"You did," I counter with a wide grin spreading across my face.

A hint of pink tints his cheeks as he attempts to divert the conversation, placing both hands gently against my chest and applying slight pressure. "Okay, okay, you look stunning right now. I might just find myself falling for you," he admits, his tone softening with a touch of vulnerability.

"You might?" I ask teasingly, raising my eyebrow.

The exchange between us begins as light-hearted banter, filled with playful jabs and teasing remarks. However, the atmosphere shifts subtly when I notice a faint blush creeping across Ishan's ears. "Your ears—" Before things could escalate, Ishan pushes me away gently, reminding me about our lateness. "We're going to be late."

"Huh ah-.......yes late" I stammer with my words a little as I realise how close we were and -

After calming a little I see Ishan taking a shirt out and I realise that the shirt I brought for him yesterday, I forget to give him. "Wait a minute" I say approaching the cupboard and taking out the shopping bag and giving it to him he was confused as why I am giving anything to him but when he opened it he realised that it's the same shirt that I was asking him to buy.

"This shirt ––" he begins, his gaze shifting between me and the clothing in his hands.

"Yes, I bought it for you," I confess with a mischievous grin,

"You didn't have to," he protest though a flicker of gratitude shine in his eyes.

"But I wanted to," I insist, my tone softening with sincerity. "Try it on. I have a feeling it will suit you perfectly."

When he emerges wearing that sleek black satin shirt, the sight leaves me awestruck. He rolls the sleeves of the shirt upwards, his hair is set with a few strands cascading effortlessly across his forehead.

He rolls one sleeve, and before he can move to do the other, I take the opportunity. With a gentle touch, I hold his other sleeve and roll it up, revealing his tattoos underneath. He lets me do it without saying anything. All this time, we hold each other's gaze.

I fix his gold chain. "My choice is really good," I declare with a satisfied smile. He looks toward me with a hint of amusement in his expression."I mean the shirt, it's my choice, right?" I say teasingly. He acts as if he knew I was talking about the shirt and not about anything else."Of course, you mean the shirt," he responds, his tone a little defensive but cute.

After getting ready, we all meet in Surya Bhai's room. He, Siraj Bhai, and Shreya are already there, where Surya Bhai himself is practically shining in his over-the-top shiny coat.

The mission? To sneak out of the hotel without attracting any unwanted attention. Easy, right? Well, not quite.

As luck would have it (or rather, not have it), we get caught on our way out by none other than Rohit and Virat Bhaiya. Virat Bhaiya gives me this long, perplexed look and says, "Why do you look like you're about to tie the knot?" I almost blurt out that I'm actually on my way to break up a wedding, but thankfully, I bite my tongue.

We didn't think about getting caught, so we don't have any good excuses either. All five of us start looking at each other for some believable answers when Shreyas, God bless his soul, comes up with a brilliant idea: "vo bhaiya, Shubman has a photoshoot, that's why he is wearing this."

"Huh, a photoshoot?" Virat Bhaiya raises his eyebrows, clearly skeptical.

"Yes, bhaiya," I say following Shreyas's script.

"But how did you get a shoot here? No one knew that we were going to stay here," Virat Bhaiya questions, grilling me about the logistics.

"Um, actually, I don't know how I got this shoot. Darshan called me and told me about it, so..." I trail off, hoping Virat Bhaiya is somewhat satisfied with my answer.

Then, the moment of glory arrives: "By the way, you are looking handsome" Jksksksksksksks Virat Bhaiya actually compliments me on my looks. I grin like a Cheshire cat – being praised by The Virat Kohli himself is a major achievement.

But just when we thought we were in the clear, Rohit Bhaiya has to  ruin it by asking, "You have your shoot, but why are these guys so ready as if they're going to a party?" He says pointing toward Ishan, Shreyas, Siraj, and Surya Bhai. Siraj Bhaiya's attempt to save the day by saying, "We are going to support Shubi," his futile attempt meets with raised eyebrows and incredulous stares.

"What?" Rohit Bhaiya raised his eyebrows, listening to the absurd lie Siraj Bhaiya just spat out.

Ishan leaned his head towards Siraj Bhaiya a little and with an awkward smile, he whispered, "Bhaiya, thoda believable jhoot bol le ?"

Surya Bhaiya, ever the smooth operator, tried to salvage the situation. He said, "I have a friend here, and he invited us for lunch."

Virat Bhaiya looked at his watch and said, "Lunch? At 3 pm?"

"Um, it was supposed to be lunch, but we are a little late. He'll still host us, don't worry," Shreyas took the matter into his hands.

"Why would I worry?" scoffed Virat Bhaiya.

Virat and Rohit Bhaiya, not entirely convinced but apparently tired of our shenanigans, let us off the hook. And with that, we finally made our escape. Once outside, Surya Bhai couldn't contain his laughter at Siraj bhaiya's feeble attempt "Seriously, Siraj? 'We are going to support Shubi'... What a hilarious lie."

Shreyas chimed in too, reliving the moment when Rohit Bhaiya's expression was pure gold. "Did you see Rohit Bhai's face when he said that? I thought we were going to have to tell them the truth, but you saved our asses."

Surya Bhai did an imaginary hair flip.

---

We reached the venue, and it's been decided that all four of them will go inside first, and I'll make my entry at the correct moment, stealing the spotlight. Inside, nerves are getting to me, basicallyandar se fatti padi hainbut I'll act brave

I receive Ishan's message commanding me to make my entry.

With a determined stride, I push open the huge gates that are already ajar, announcing my arrival to the room.

"Stop!" I exclaim, my voice cutting through the crowd, drawing all eyes to me.

Let the drama begin.

~~~

Title: Chapter : 26
Chapter_Text:
•SHUBMAN GILL•

We reached the venue, and it's been decided that all four of them will go inside first, and I'll make my entry at the correct moment, stealing the spotlight. Inside, nerves are getting to me, basicallyandar se fatti padi hainbut I'll act brave

I receive Ishan's message commanding me to make my entry.

With a determined stride, I push open the huge gates that are already ajar, announcing my arrival to the room.

"Stop!" I exclaim, my voice cutting through the crowd, drawing all eyes to me.

Let the drama begin.

I walk with an even pace, the crowd giving me way, everyone looks quite shocked because I am wearing the groom's attire. With each step, my heart pounds in my chest. It feels like the first time you go on stage in front of a big crowd to give your speech.

With all eyes on me, I make a beeline for the altar.

As I make my way forward, out of the corner of my eye, I spot them standing off to the side. Three of them wear expressions of pure delight, while Ishan's expression is more stoic.

As I approach the ceremony zone, my gaze falls on Ellisa. She is in her wedding gown, and opposite to her stands the groom, who is wearing similar attire to mine.

In the background, I can hear the murmur of shocked people. I give a glance towards someone whom I assume is Ellisa's father, and trust me, if I had seen him before, I would not have agreed to any of this because it looks like if he is going to punch me, then I am not going to wake up for 2 days. He looks intimidating, a bit too much.

I stand in front of Ellisa, and I see relief wash over her face. "Ellisa," I say with a fake hurt expression, "what are you doing?"

I can sense the stares of puzzled people behind me.

"Didn't... didn't you promise to marry me?" Ellisa tries to look bewildered, but all I can see is relief on her face.

The groom takes a step towards me and says, "Who are you?" I kid you not, he is literally getting offended in lowercase.

By the way he is behaving, it seems like either he knows about this or he doesn't really care. Because if someone showed up at my wedding in a freaking groom attire, I would be really mad. But he seems quite chill.

With confidence, I reply, "I am someone whom she promised to marry. Now, tell me, who are you?" Smooth, right? Wrong. It's very lame, but no worries because whatever he says next is equally lame.

"I am someone whom she is going to marry," his response is as if reading from the script of clichés.

I come a bit closer to Ellisa, gently holding her hand, and with mock hurt, I exclaim, "What is happening, Ellisa? Are you cheating on me?"

"Uh, I... um," she stammers, trying to form a coherent sentence, but before she can say anything, her father advances towards me,Please, sir, don't punch me.

Giving me a once-over look, he then turns to his daughter and inquires, "Ellisa, is this your boyfriend? I thought you just made up stuff to avoid marriage and your boyfriend is non existent" Despite his intimidating demeanor, there is a hint of gentleness in his voice.

"I assure you, sir, I am very much real," I declare, straightening my bow tie in a feeble attempt to appear more composed. The murmurs in the background grow louder, and when I glance back, I catch sight of Surya Bhai giving me a thumbs-up.

"Dad, I've been trying to tell you, but you never believed me. Now, I've made up my mind. I'm marrying him," Ellisa says, gesturing towards the groom.

"You can't do this to me, Ellisa. Were all those promises you made to me fake?" I mutter, my voice filled with mock hurt.

Now, what is wild to me is that the groom isn't doing or saying anything. So I am just assuming that he knows everything.

Ellisa keeps stealing glances toward the main gate, clearly waiting for her boyfriend to arrive. And me? Well, I am waiting too, wondering how this bizarre drama will unfold.

"You don't have to do this, Ellisa," a bridesmaid speaks, coming towards Ellisa. "If you love him," she gestures towards me, "then you should marry him."

Ellisa's mother also chimes in, "We never want to pressure you, dear. If you've got someone you love, and most importantly, who loves you back, well, we don't have any problem marrying you off to them."

I don't think Ellisa even listens to half of the things they say because her entire focus is towards the entrance.

Finally, the groom speaks, surprising me with his unexpected words. "Well, if Ellisa loves you, who am I to stand in her way?" He gestures for me to take his place.

"You come here with the intention of marrying our daughter, then let's do it!" he exclaims with enthusiasm in his voice. "And look at you, already dressed to impress in that groom's suit! Everything's perfect!"

Perfect? Nothing is perfect sir, but before I can muster any sentence, he affectionately pats my shoulder. The weight of his hand feels heavy.

But wait a damn minute, why are they all so quick to agree to this bizarre turn of events? And why, oh why, did we think it was a brilliant idea to make my grand entrance in a groom's tuxedo?

Reality hits me hard: they actually want me to marry her. Panic sets in faster than a bullet train, and I desperately scan the room for any sign of Ellisa's boyfriend. But, there is no sign of him.

As I stare into her eyes, I see a mix of confusion and anxiety mirroring my own. The situation is as awkward as it gets. I mean, what do you do when you find yourself in the middle of a wedding ceremony you never signed up for? Should I scream "prank!" and hope for the best?

Before I can come up with a new plan, her father ushers me to stand in front of Ellisa. I look toward my people for some kind of help. I see Sreyas, Siraj, and Surya Bhai saying something to Ishan. I couldn't make out their expressions, but I can only hope that  they are concocting an escape plan and not discussing what they are having for dinner, because sometimes the collective braincell count of the gang can go to literally one.

Ellisa and I both look at each other with helpless ness in our eyes. One of the bridesmaids gives the ring to her, and as soon as she takes the ring, a voice comes from somewhere: "You can't do this to me--"

And everyone's attention shifts toward the source of the voice.

It's Ishan. He emerges from the sea of guests, and I sigh. Seeing him coming to my rescue makes me feel relieved a bit.

Title: Chapter : 27
Chapter_Text:
"Oh really? Then prove it," she demands. "Prove that he is your boyfriend." The bridesmaid challenges Ishan.

"How?"

"I don't know, do something... kiss him."

Ishan's demeanor momentarily breaks at her words, but he conceals it quickly.

I have to intervene now. I try to say something, "Listen—" but before I can say anything further, I hear the swift footsteps of Ishan approaching me.

Before I can make sense of anything, with a bold gesture, he seizes the lapel of my tuxedo, pulling me towards him. And then, he presses his lips to mine.

In this moment, something ignites within me, and the world around me fades away.

The voices around me grow louder, but at this moment, they are like mere whispers fading into a distant hum. The emotions I am feeling are too overwhelming, and I can only hear the steady thumping of my own heart at this point.

I can't think of anything other than the feel of his soft lips, gently pressed against mine but unmoving, my heart going wild in its cage.

I dare to move my lips against his, capturing his lips in a slow and tender kiss, but he doesn't reciprocate.

But when he does, my heart skips a beat, or maybe more than just a beat. When his lips move against mine, it feels nothing like the dream I had about him, where he kissed me – far from it. This kiss is something I didn't know I needed. This kiss feels like even if I have to give up my most prized possession just to feel his lips against mine, I'll do that in a heartbeat.

Everything starts making a lot of sense, every feeling I get looking at him, every pang of jealousy I feel whenever I see him with someone else, the constant urge to be around him, to touch him, to snuggle with him, suddenly falls into place like pieces of a puzzle.

And in this moment, I realize that I am in love with him. I am in love with my teammate. I am in love with my friend. I am in love with my fake boyfriend.

I AM IN LOVE WITH ISHAN, and it's not good.

When the kiss breaks, I just stare at Ishan's face, though his attention is diverted somewhere else; someone just entered the room. I hear Ellisa squealing, and I hear loud gasps, but my focus is solely on Ishan's face, and my mind keeps replaying what just happened again and again.

I can still taste him on my lips, sweet and dreamy. My gaze momentarily lingers on his lips because all I want to do at this moment is lean in and kiss him again. But he probably won't appreciate that, so I control myself.

When I recover from the effect and the realization, I glance over to see Ellisa hugging someone tightly, probably her long-awaited boyfriend who finally decides to grace us all with his presence. Thank goodness he arrived. But my jaw hits the floor when they break the hug, and I see who her boyfriend is.

It's Mayank

Yes, Ishan's childhood best friend – MAYANK, the same mayank I was getting jealoused over. I can't believe my eyes. Then I look towards Ishan to see his reaction, his expression confirming my disbelief. Mayank looks just as shocked as the rest of us.

So, let me get this straight – Mayank is Ellisa's boyfriend? Well, talk about the odds."

Ellisa's father collapses into a nearby chair while clutching his heart, letting out an exaggerated gasp. "Now who is he?" he asks, on the verge of passing out.

"Okay, calm down everyone, I'll explain," she starts explaining everything.Finally, as she concludes her monologue, she reaches out and grasps Mayank's hand with a dramatic motion. "And he," she announces with joy, "is my one and only true love."

Raising an eyebrow skeptically, her father retorts, "Oh, tell me no one else will show up pretending to be your boyfriend," gaining his composure again he scoffs "I can't handle another surprise suitor popping out of nowhere claiming to be your one and only."

Ishan and Mayank had a talk in the which they share their mutual shock and talk about the absurdity of the situation.

After this, everything happens in a daze. We return to our hotel, but what starts to hurt me is that no one talks about the kiss, no one mentions it even once. And the most agonizing part is that even Ishan is playing it cool as if nothing has happened.

I had thought the gang would tease us to death for it, but no one says a word about it. No one speaks a single word on our way back to the hotel.

And I feel as if I just assumed that kiss, and it actually didn't happen at all.

In our room, everything remains the same. We talk as we used to, Ishan's demeanor betraying nothing of the emotional turmoil that I am going through. And let me tell you, dear reader, it hurts. It hurts a lot.

The next day, I find myself at the airport with Ishan. Mayank is going back to India today, and Ishan wants to see him off. Mayank hugs Ishan and then turns to me with outstretched arms. I awkwardly hug him back, and he whispers something in my ear: "Well, I am rooting for you two."

"Huh?" My brain tries to catch up with whatever he is saying.

"You know, when I was with him that night, all he did was talk about you," he says, smiling warmly at me, which I reciprocate. "Really?"

"Of course, like how clumsy you are and how much you whine at small things and how you have a knack for inviting troubles."

"Oh, please tell me there is a 'but' somewhere in between."

"Of course, there is." His smile turns mischievous. "It'll be better if you ask him about those."

Ishan tells Mayank to hurry up, and before parting ways, Mayank says to Ishan, "Take care of your boyfriend," and then he winks at me.

When I look at Ishan, all I see is him mouthing the word "fake."

Yeah, fake.

---

Next day, the whole team shifts to London, and the practice begins. It is so hard to keep focus on the ball. All I can think about is the kiss and the realization.

The first match against England goes in a haze.

And the second match.

And the third match as well.

We return to India after the series, but mentally I am still there, at that wedding hall, kissing Ishan.

~~~

Ishan, stop acting as if you didn't kiss him back. Stop acting like a bi-

(I'll pretent as if I didn't make him act like that hehe)

Title: Chapter : 28
Chapter_Text:
If anyone missed the last update then please read it, It has Their kiss and Shubman's realization of love.

•

Shubman's reaction by the end of the chapter (don't skip):

I find myself in my Mumbai flat. Partly because I wanted some time alone, but also because the BCCI event next week is in Mumbai.

We only returned from England yesterday, and since then, I haven't spoken to Ishan. Am I experiencing withdrawal symptoms? The past few months were filled with cricket matches, tours, and events, so I hardly had to be away from him.

It's pathetic. I'm pathetic.

I head into the kitchen to make myself some coffee. As I grab the coffee, it instantly reminds me of the day when Ishan showed me how he likes his coffee. He isn't a black coffee person; he likes it with milk and sugar.

He showed me how he prepares his coffee, which was interesting, but then again, anything he does is interesting to me.

He let me take a sip from his coffee, and it was really good. Now, I'm craving it.

Instead of my usual black coffee, I start making my coffee the Ishan-way. I pour the hot milk over the creamy mixture, and it looks delicious. Grabbing my phone from my room, I stroll into the living room. Sitting on the sofa, I start scrolling through news on my phone, but my focus is hardly on it. Lately, all my brain can think of is this one particular 5 feet and 6-inch tall man who probably doesn't give a shit about my feelings. Or maybe he does? I don't know.

Before I can take the first sip of the coffee, the doorbell rings. I set the coffee mug on the table to check it, and as I open the door, I see Nia and Darshan standing there with some stuff in their hands. Nia is breathing heavily..

I step aside to let them in, both of them dumps the stuff on the table. Darshan leaves quickly, but Nia stays, still breathing heavily. "Water," she manages to say inbetween her breaths.

I hand her a glass of cold water. After gulping it down in one go, she asks for more. After downing another glass, she plops down on the sofa. "Why isn't the lift in your building working?" Her breathing starts to normalize.

"I don't know. I didn't even realize it wasn't working," I say, noticing her narrowed eyes. "You haven't been anywhere?" she questions.

"Not since this morning," I reply, taking a seat in front of her.

She relaxes herself and leans back on the sofa. "I should join the gym."

"Yes, you should," I agree.

She gives me a glare.

"What are all these?" I ask, pointing towards the stuff they dumped on the table.

"They are fan gifts," she answers.

"Oh."

She then sits up straight, her face morphing into an excited expression. "You know what? For the BCCI event next week, I think it would be fun if both of you wear matching outfits." She taps something on her phone. "Boyfriends should be twinning," she adds.

I sigh. "I don't want to continue it." Her head snaps a bit too fast, and I'm afraid she might have sprained her neck.

"But we can't do that, we have ODI World Cup (khada hoon Aaj bhi wahi)coming later this year, we can't afford any negative PR before that." She pauses, a bit hesitant to say anything further but then she continues anyway "we can do something after that, like release some statement."

"No," I protest. "I don't mean that... I mean... I mean..." I struggle to find the correct words.

"Yeah?" she prompts me to continue.

I bury my face in my hands and speak in a low voice, "I don't want the wordfaketo be there."

Silence.

I glance up to confirm that she is still there, looking at me with confusion, and then slowly the realization dawns upon her. "You mean... you have feelings? For him?"

I nod slowly.

She clasps her hand over her mouth and squeals. SHE LITERALLY SQUEALS.

"Oh my god, oh my god, for real?" she asks me excitedly.

Whatever reaction I was expecting from her, this one wasn't one of them.

"My ship is sailing!" she exclaims joyously.

"Huh?"

"I already see the chemistry between you two. It's a shame that it took you so long to realize."

"Well, in that case, it's more like your ship is sinking because I am the only one with feelings."

"And why are you so confident about it?"

I explain the whole thing to her, how we helped Ellisa and how Ishan kissed me, and how he never mentioned it afterwards. She enjoys every bit of it.

And when I tell her about the kiss, her eyes go comically wide. "You two had your first kiss!" she exclaims, clearly ecstatic. "I'm sad that so many people witnessed that, but I didn't," she says with a mock hurt expression. "I can't believe I missed all that drama. I should have been there."

"Yeah, so you could have made us both marry each other right there," I scoff lightly.

"Of course."

Now her whole demeanor changes, and she looks pointedly at me. "Did you tell him?"

"What? No... How can I when I know the feeling isn't mutual?"

"How can you say it so confidently? In my opinion, you should definitely tell Ishan about your feelings. He deserves to know." A smile spreads on her face. "And who knows, he might feel the same for you?"

"Not possible."

She tries to say something, but I cut her off, changing the topic. "How is it going with Darshan?"

She rolls her eyes. "Fuck him."

With a mischievous smile, I retort, "Well, that's your job."

_____

The BCCi even went smooth and we are currently in Hyderabad for the tri series against New Zealand.

The first match ended up in our favour, after the match, I went out with shreyas and and miyaan.

Title: Chapter : 29
Chapter_Text:
Note : After this chapter we will going to have Ishan's POV in which we will come back to the confession part again, so aaj ka chapter padhne ke baad koi gaali nhi dega-

••

•SHUBMAN GILL•

I carefully plant a soft kiss on his forehead. "Ishan, I lo-" I hesitate, my heart pounding in my chest. "I really like you, Ishan," I finally say.

I should have felt relieved to confess to him, but to my pure horror, he slowly opens his eyes, and I quickly shut mine.

WHAT THE FUCK.WHAT THE FUCK.WHAT THE FUUUUUCCCCKKKKKKK.

I just want to un-exist myself at this moment.

Was he awake all along? How much did he hear? Did he catch just the last part? Even if he only heard the last part, it's bad enough. Let alone the whole thing.

But this is unfair. I said all those things because I thought he was sleeping.

My heart feels ready to explode, pounding at a dangerously high rate. My eyes remain closed because I can't afford to open them, can't afford to see him staring into my soul. What is he thinking right now? Is he disgusted with me?

These thoughts keep swirling in my mind, but I don't open my eyes. I just can't. Not after the monologue I just gave him, which he happened to hear but wasn't supposed to.

A few moments pass, and I feel a touch on my cheek. Thank God, it's not a slap, but a gentle caress. Then, I feel a feathery kiss on my forehead. It's so gentle I might be imagining it. And then,  it's gone. The touch vanishes as quickly as it came.

Did I just imagine that? Is my brain playing tricks on me now? Maybe I'm hallucinating from the sheer embarrassment.

My eyes remain shut. It took sleep hours, like literal, actual hour to saunter over to me. And when it finally decided to grace me with its presence, what do I dream about? Him. Just him. Talk about being whipped.

The next morning, I wake up to find he's already gone. Now, keep in mind, during all our time sharing a room, he's never once woken up before me or got out of the bed before me. Not even close. But today, oh today, he decides to make an exception and slip out without even bothering to wake me up.

Speaks volumes, doesn't it?

I know I'm late for practice, but for a few more minutes, I just sit on the bed staring into the distance, replaying last night's happenings. My confession, him popping open his eyes, as if saying: 'Surprise, MF!'

How I wish it had all been a dream. But no, the empty feeling is settling in like an unwelcome houseguest. He knows. He knows now. He knows that I know that he knows.

I don't want any confrontation. Not now, not ever. I'm scared, actually scared. This is not how I pictured confessing my feelings to someone, it wasn't romantic it was a 'deer-in-headlights' moment.

_____________

During the practice session, Ishan keeps glancing in my direction. It's painfully obvious he wants to have a conversation with me. Of course he does. I can already hear it:"Oh my God, Gill, I am so sorry. I can't reciprocate your feelings. You know I'm not gay or bi, right? It's just tragic you fell for me. I mean, I get it, I'm quite charismatic, but you shouldn't have let this happen. You should move on... Awww, don't cry. Let me give you a hug. But wait, what if giving you a hug makes you fall even harder for me? Should I just slap you so you can move on? Trust me, it won't hurt much."

Or even worse:"Tch tch tch, Gill? Seriously? All this time, you weren't looking at me as a friend but as something else? All your gestures had ulterior motives? Wait, let me ask Rohit bhaiya to change our rooms. I can't believe you were romanticizing me all this time. I agreed to be your fake boyfriend, but I think you weren't satisfied with that. Now you want me as your real boyfriend? Shakal dekhi hain apni? Aukat me rahe aap thoda"(Have you seen your face? Stay in your lane, man.)

As I am considering all the possibilities, someone snakes their arm around me. It's Hardik Bhaiya.

"Kya ho gya? Kahan khoya hua hai?"

"Uh, ummm, nothing, Bhaiya."

"Waise, main dekh raha hoon, tum dono saath mein kam dikhte ho.  ladai hui hai? Kya?"

"Kon?" I ask, feigning innocence.

He lightly hits my head, "Abbey tere ko pata hain kon, Tera boyfriend aur kon?"

"Nhi, hamari ladai nhi hui, aur vaise bhi team me we should bond with everyone"

"Haan haanWE sHOULd bOND WiTh EveRyOneke baache tab ye gyaan kaha gya tha jab usse dinbhar chipka rehta tha?"

Just as I'm about to reply something, Rahul bhaiya summons him, phew, thank god for his timely interruption.

I steal another glance at Ishan, only to find him fully engrossed in his practice. I let out a sigh, attempting to redirect my focus back to my own practice... emphasis on the word 'attempt.'

During the break, as I go to take some refreshments, I notice Ishan approaching me. Is he intentionally coming towards me, or is he just coming here for the refreshments? What should I do now?

In an desperate attempt to hide myself I crouch between the dugout chairs, concealing myself from his sight but how can be luck on my side in these situations?

*Achoooooo* I sneeze a bit too loudly

FUCK ME BECAUSE WHAT THE FUCK?

Between the gaps, I catch sight of Ishan. He is striding towards me, and my heart starts beating a bit too fast. I seriously need to see a doctor because at this rate, I am definitely going to have some serious heart issues. Like, the literal ones.

Oh no, oh no, he is coming in my direction, I feel a surge of panic.WHAT SHOULD I DO NOW? But maybe luck is slightly on my side today. "Ishan!" Rahul bhaiya shouts from the field. This is the second time Rahul bhaiya has come to my rescue without even knowing it. I could practically kiss his feet right now, but then Ishan might see me, so I have to drop the plan. Also, they are dirty because of the on-field practice.

Ishan takes a U-turn, and I take a few deep breaths, lying down here for a few more minutes to make sure Ishan is actually gone and no one will see me emerging from between the dugout chairs. I get up and dust off my clothes.

What has my life become within just two weeks? Life was beautiful when I living in blissful ignorance. But now, it feels like I am walking on eggshells around Ishan. Sometimes I just want to go to him and say,"I love you, so what? What are you going to do?"But only if I had the guts.

Time passes slowly, it is agonizing now, I keep peeking in his direction. I can't focus on anything.

It's just frustrating.

__________

I am going to stay in Shrey's room for a while, or until Ishan sleeps, but the problem is I have to pass my own room for that, and I can clearly see that the door is ajar.

I just have to keep walking until I reach Shrey's room.

As I am passing my room, something stops me in my tracks. I can sense that something is ongoing there. My brain urges me to look, while my heart says to keep going.

Against my better judgment, I peek through the ajar door, and what I see makes my heart drop to the pit of the earth. I should not have stopped; I should have kept walking. I really should have, because what I am witnessing-I should not be witnessing that, not after the realization. Both of my palms clench into tight fists without my consent. My heartbeat races erratically. There is still time; I should leave. I should just go to Shrey's room because I don't have the heart to see what is going to happen next.

(Let's go a few hours back)

I am within earshot distance from him as someone approaches him and whispers, "Someone came to meet you." I hear it "in the stands." When I glance over, I see a bunch of people there, most of them fans who are here to see the practice and to meet their favorites.

So, most probably the "someone" in question is his fan and wanted to meet him. No big deal.

And then I see him talking to someone. A girl is speaking to him animatedly, and there is an undeniable energy between them that piques my curiosity. With a bit of discreet inquiry, I learn she's not just a fan, she's his neighbor and a friend as well, her name is Aanya.

For some strange reason, I don't feel jealous of her. Currently, she has all his attention, and she is saying something I can't hear because, of course, I am on the ground, and they are in the stands.

(Back to present)

Aanya is kneeling, one hand outstretched, holding a ring, she is proposing to him, As if this alone isn't enough to shatter my heart, my gaze drifts to Ishan. When I see the smile spread across his face, a smile that signifies his approval, I never imagined that his smile would ever pierce my heart like this. The smile that I love to see on his face is now shattering my soul.

I realize there is nothing in my life that could have prepared me for this moment.To say that I am hurt is like doing injustice to what I am feeling right now.

I fight the urge to run out from here and never know what happened next, but for some reason, my body decides to torture me more as it stays glued to its place.

And when I see his smile turning wider and cheekier as he accepts the ring from her hand and holds it on his own, the world drops from underneath me.

I find my steps going backwards involuntarily. The only thing that I can hear is my own heartbeat. Maybe someone called me, but my body didn't stop. I turn around and I just keep walking and walking mindlessly.

Yeah, I deserve this. I deserve this devastation, this pain, this hurt, everything. I deserve this for letting my feelings slip, for not having control over my own feelings.

You have a girlfriend, you never told me about her. Of course, why would you? But why did you kiss me? You shouldn't have. To know how your lips taste and never be able to feel them against mine ever again, it's just pure torture.

After I don't know how many hours of walking, I find myself sitting on a bench, it starts to drizzle. A girl and a boy stroll along a quiet street, laughing over something. The boy reaches into his bag and takes out an umbrella. He opens it, draping his arm over her shoulder, pulling her closer. She nestles closer to him, possibly blushing. Their fingers intertwine as they look at each other, smiles playing on their lips. The soft orange glow of the street lamp casts ethereal shadows across their faces as they walk like this, soon vanishing from my sight. And then I break down into tears.

~~~

Title: Chapter : 30
Chapter_Text:
•ISHAN KISHAN•

She approaches me with dramatic steps, her curly hair bouncing with each movement. As she reaches me, she genuflects, offering me a ring, and mimicking, "Will you be the chocolate of my hot chocolate?"

I smile at her antics, stretching out my hand to take the ring from her. It's a beautiful diamond ring.

"Can you believe he literally proposed to me like that in front of everyone?" She stands up, and I hand the ring back to her. She slips it onto her ring finger.

"It's cute, though," I remark.

"It was embarrassing," she says, rolling her eyes.

This is Aanya. She used to be my neighbor,her parents and mine are close friends, and she's like a sister to me. Currently, she's living here in Hyderabad. When she heard I was in town for my match, she came to see me during practice and now she's here, narrating the whole story about how her boyfriend proposed to her.

"Where is Shubman?" she asks abruptly. "Your boyfriend."

Shubman. Yes, where is he? Is he going to ignore me again? Earlier this evening, as I was heading to the dressing room, I saw him hiding between the dugout chairs. It was hilarious because, with his tall figure, he was impossible to miss.

"Maybe he's with the teammates," I say, dialing his number. "Let me call him."

Once.Twice.Thrice.

He doesn't pick up.

I give Aanya an awkward smile. "He might be busy somewhere."

"Oh," her smile dims. "I really wanted to meet him."

"It's okay. We're here for two more days, so I'll make sure you meet him."

She nods. "Okay, gotta go then."

She gives me a hug and leaves. I stare at my phone.He needs to stop doing this. To me.

After two more hours of waiting for him, I knock on Shrey's door.

"Is Shubman with you?" I ask, peeking behind him to get a look, but I see no one.

"No, I haven't seen him since practice," Shrey replies.

"Oh," I say, my disappointment evident.

Shrey senses it and asks, "Everything's fine, right?"

"Yeah, everything is fine," I lie, though my heart feels otherwise. "He must be strolling somewhere."

Everything is fine except my heart.

I dial Shubman's number one more time. The phone rings, but he doesn't pick up. He did this last night as well.

This morning, when I woke up, I found myself snuggled against him. It wasn't unusual, but I discovered something: his hold was never strong. It was just me who didn't want to get away from the warmth, so I pretended I couldn't get up.

I've done pretty questionable things, and this is just another one to add to the list.

______

"Aahhh," I whimper, holding my neck as soon as I wake up. Taking in my awkward position, I realize I fell asleep last night waiting for him again. How long is he going to play this game of ignoring me?

But but Something is off. I scan the room, still holding my neck to avoid the pain from movement. Everything is as it was last night.

I don't see his phone or watch on the side table. Did he sleep somewhere else? When is this boy going to grow up and stop doing silly things?

It's possible he woke up early and left for breakfast, but why was I in such an awkward position in bed? Didn't he try to ease me up? Is he upset with me?

But he isn't even talking to me, so how can he be upset with me? I should be the one upset with him.

I check the bathroom, confirming my suspicion-he didn't come here. He didn't sleep here, nor did he shower here.

Then Where is he?

I quickly dial his number, but this time it says switched off. He might have forgotten to charge it, and the battery drained out.

I shoot a quick message in the team group asking about him. Everyone replies almost immediately, but the responses are not what I expect. No one knows where Shubman is, and that's not a good sign.

Where the hell is this boy?

I ask everyone to meet at the dining hall. Nobody thinks it's worrisome that Shubman isn't here because it's normal for team members to wander the city on their own. But what's not normal is that no one has seen him since last evening, and no one knows his whereabouts. And his phone is now switched off.

When I share these details, everyone's expressions turn grim. Kuldeep bhaiya is the first to speak. "I saw him leaving the hotel last evening shortly after we returned from practice." Everyone turns their attention to him. "I even called him, but he didn't stop as if he didn't hear me."

"Did anyone see him after that?" asks Rohit bhaiya, looking around.

We all shake our heads.

"Why did he leave the hotel, and where did he go?" Virat bhaiya asks, more to himself than anyone else, and no one has an answer.

Suddenly, Jassi bhaiya's head snaps toward Rohit bhaiya. "Wait, I think I saw him last night," he says urgently, trying to recollect his memory, "Maybe near the pool area. He was going toward the back of the hotel."

"Are you sure it was Shubman?" Hardik bhaiya questions.

"I saw his back, but it was him," Jassi bhaiya replies with enough certainty.

"And what time was it?" inquires Virat bhaiya.

"I think it was around 9 or 9:30ish."

A moment of silence follows as we try to process this information. Then Shrey speaks, catching me off guard. "But if he was in the hotel, why didn't he go to his room, which he is sharing with Ishan?" His question is directed at me.

What am I supposed to answer? Tell them that he confessed to me the day before yesterday, and now he thinks I'll eat him alive if he comes near me or breathes the same air?

I try to deflect the question. "I don't know. I called him thrice yesterday, but he didn't pick up. I waited for him quite late but then dozed off. This morning, his phone was switched off."

The tension in the room increases with each passing minute. I feel guilty-what if he is doing all this because of me?

Rohit bhaiya turns to me. "We should ask his manager. Maybe he knows something. Do you have his number?"

I hadn't thought of that. "No, I don't, but I can ask my manager for Darshan's number."

A few minutes later, I receive Darshan's number and dial it.

"Hello, Darshan. Do you know where Shubman is? Did he call you? Does he have any shoot or event to attend here? Did you two meet yesterday or today?" I ask in one breath without giving him a chance to speak.

"Calm down, Ishan. What happened?" His voice sounds as if I've disturbed him during an important meeting. "The last contact I had with him was two days ago, and no, he didn't have any shoot in Hyderabad."

Oh no. Darshan was our last hope, and he also doesn't know about Shubman. Now things are getting a bit too serious for my liking.

"Darshan, we can't find him, and his phone is switched off too."

Virat bhaiya asks for the phone, so I hand it to him. "Hello, Darshan. Can you please find out if he has any friends living here where he might have gone, or any place in particular where he might go? ...Okay... Please hurry." He cuts the call and hands the phone back to me.

"We should check the hotel ourselves. What if he is here?" suggests Kuldeep bhaiya.

Right. Jassi bhaiya claims he saw him in the hotel last night, so there's a high chance he's still here.

Everyone agrees and starts going in different directions.

"I swear to God, if I find him dozed off somewhere in the hotel, I am going to kick his ass," Hardik bhaiya says, his tone a mix of worry and frustration.

"Not before me," chimes in Virat bhaiya.

Not before me, I say internally.

After the 'Shubman Hunt,' which ends in disappointment judging by everyone's expressions, we all gather in Virat bhaiya's room, now all looking tense and worried.

"Everyone, don't panic," Virat bhaiya starts, though he looks more panicked than anyone. "What if he went to some place nearby without informing us, and then his phone died?" He tries to sound convincing.

"Yes," Rohit bhaiya supports his theory. "We should go out and check, and if we can't find him, then..." An ominous pause. We all know what he's going to say. "Then we'll file a complaint."

We are on our way out of the hotel when my phone rings. It's Darshan. He informs me that Shubman doesn't have any friends living here, but he gives me the names of two or three places where Shubman might have gone.

Quickly, we divide ourselves into three groups of five and start our search.

~~~

Shubman stop stripping yourself in club and get back to your man -

•

Life is throwing lemonsBut lemonade nahi ban Raha -

This is the last drama of the book after this everything will fall into place and happy ending!

Title: Chapter : 31
Chapter_Text:
•ISHAN KISHAN•

We are on our way out of the hotel when my phone rings. It's Darshan. He informs me that Shubman doesn't have any friends living here, but he gives me the names of two or three places where Shubman might have gone.

Quickly, we divide ourselves into three groups of five and start our search.

I'm with Rohit, Virat, Kuldeep, and Hardik bhaiya. Disguised with masks, caps, and sunglasses, we search nearby restaurants and popular spots. But there’s no sign of him. Fans keep recognizing us, asking for autographs and selfies, which slows us down. We can't even show his picture and ask if anyone has seen him. It’s complete chaos.

We miss practice today, but that’s the least of our worries. I keep calling his number, hoping he's charged his phone somewhere.

As evening approaches, there’s still no information about him. A sense of dread starts to settle in. I try to push the thoughts away, but I can’t help it. Where is he? Why is his phone off? Why hasn't he returned? What has happened to him?

If only I had insisted on talking to him, maybe he wouldn't have left. It's my fault, and if something terrible has happened to him, I will never forgive myself. I just- My stream of thoughts is suddenly interrupted by Kuldeep Bhaiya's voice, "Are you crying?" I look at him, confused. "Huh?" I touch my cheek and feel a tear. I hadn't even noticed it falling.

Kuldeep bhaiya’s words catch me off guard, and as the other three turn to look at me, I quickly wipe away a tear.

Rohit bhaiya’s phone rings. From his tone, it’s clear he’s speaking to our coach. We hadn't informed anyone before leaving the hotel, so now Rohit bhaiya is explaining everything.

After hanging up, he turns to us, his expression serious. “Coach says we should return to the hotel. Going to the police station ourselves won’t help, it will just draw unwanted attention.”

We all nod in agreement. “We’ll send someone from the managing team to the police station,” he continues. “If necessary, we can request the police to come to the hotel to inquire about him. We can’t let the media find out that Shubman is missing.” Again, we nod.

Rohit bhaiya looks at our worried faces and tries to reassure us. “Don’t worry, he’ll be okay.”

I want so badly to believe him, but the circumstances are making it difficult for me.

“I’m going to inform the others,” Virat bhaiya says, picking up his phone to relay the plan to the rest of the team.

_______

Late at night, the police arrivs at the hotel after Darshan registered the FIR. Initially, he wasn’t supposed to come here today, but after learning about the situation, he flew in immediately.

Given the high-profile nature of the case, the police bring along two detectives. After gathering as much information as they can from us, they requests to check the CCTV footage from the hotel. According to Jassi bhaiya, the last place Shubman was seen was here.

Only a few of us were allowed in the security room with the police. Rohit bhaiya, Virat bhaiya, Jassi bhaiya, and I accompanies them.

As the security personnel begin reviewing the footage from the previous night, a clip from 9:17 PM catches our attention.

“Stop,” Jassi bhaiya instructs the technician, pointing at a figure on the screen. “This is Shubman. It was the last time I saw him.” the footage only shows his back as he was going toward the pool area.

One of the detectives asks, “Are you sure, Mr. Bumrah?” Jassi bhaiya nods in confirmation.

“Wait a minute, can you please zoom in a little?” I requests. The technician zoomed in, causing the image to blur slightly, but we can still make out the shirt the figure is wearing.

“This shirt,” I say, pointing at the screen, “it’s not Shubman’s.”

“But what if he went out and bought it yesterday?” Jassi bhaiya interjects.

Though it is possible, something inside me insists that the person we are looking at isn't Shubman, even though he looks strikingly similar.

We were about to move to the next footage when Virat bhaiya interrupts, “What is this? Isn’t that a tattoo?” he asks, instructing the technician to zoom in on the figure’s hand. When he does, a tattoo is clearly visible on that person's hand.

The detective turns to us. “And Mr. Gill doesn’t have any tattoo there?”

“No, he doesn’t,” I replied.

“But he does look like Shubman from behind, if we ignore the tattoo,” Jassi bhaiya clarifies. “That’s why I got confused yesterday.”

“So no one has seen him since last evening, and Mr. Kuldeep was the last one to see him before he disappeared?” ask the other detective.

We all nod. He has been missing for over 24 hours. The weight of this revelation sink into my heart. All I want to know at this moment that he is safe,

Wherever you are, please be safe.

The detective then requests footage from the outside cameras. When they shows us, it reveals Shubman leaving the hotel at 5:21 PM, wearing a white tee, the kind he usually wore under his practice jersey.

As I focus on his movements and body language, he looked lost and sad. He isn't like this during practice. Sure, he maintained his distance from me, but other than that, he seemed okay. However, the Shubman in the footage doesn't look okay at all. This is making me more scared than I already am.

The police announces they would check the CCTV footage from the surrounding area in the direction Shubman has gone. They assures us they would keep us informed of any findings.

The coaches and senior players used their connections to pressure the police and other authorities to prioritize the case while keeping it under wraps.

Everyone is distressed. We all know nobody is going to sleep tonight, but after a five-minute speech from Virat bhaiya telling  that shubman will be fine and urging us to take care of ourselves and rest, everyone start retreating to their respective rooms.

On my way back, I see Nia hugging Darshan, who gently wraps his arms around her. They both look deeply sad. Nia has been a tremendous help in the investigation today. She shares a unique bond with Shubman and clearly cares a lot about him. Darshan does too, even though he rarely shows it.

For a fleeting moment, I envy them. Nia has Darshan to turn to for solace in these hard times. But the person I want to hug right now, the person whose warmth I crave, is missing.

~~~

Title: Chapter : 32
Chapter_Text:
Chapter 32 - Shubman's husb-......boyfr-....... Best friend yeah best friend for a reason!

••

•ISHAN KISHAN•

In my room, I find myself glancing at the door every five minutes, hoping that Shubman might burst in unexpectedly, and tell me some wild story about how he got stuck with elephants or something. I can almost hear his voice, filled with excitement and laughter, before he pulls me into a tight hug and whispers that he missed me.

I gaze at the empty space beside me, he always insisted on piling two pillows under his head, even though it often meant waking up with a sore neck. The sight of the vacant spot tugs at my heart, a reminder of his absence.

I still remember the time when he made me question my sexuality- I was straight, wasn't I? But when he came into my life as my fake boyfriend, everything I thought I knew about myself began to change.

(Reference - When Ishan was heading to Shubman's house for a Pooja, he got into an accident. Ishan wasn't hurt, but the other person was hospitalized)

I still remember the time when he held me close on the bed, my tears soaking his t-shirt, I was a crying mess but he provided me the warmth of his embrace, the way he whispered soothing words, remains etched in my memory. I can still feel the tenderness of his lips when he planted a gentle kiss on my forehead, that spoke volumes of his affection and care.

___

I recall the conversation with Mayank vividly.

"What's going on between you two?" he asked, his eyes narrowing with curiosity.

"Between who?" I replied, feigning ignorance.

"You and Shubman," he said pointedly.

"Nothing," I insisted, but my voice lacked conviction.

"Come on, Ishan," Mayank pressed. "Didn't you see how jealous he got when you mentioned that I'm your best friend? And when you were treating my cut?"

I had noticed. I noticed everything-- the subtle shifts in Shubman's demeanor, the flash of jealousy in his eyes. Yet a part of me kept insisting that I was imagining things, seeing something that wasn't really there.

I remember the times when he would glance at me from across the room,

how his face fell when I mentioned I was going out with Mayank, the disappointment written all over him.

When he called me to come back, stating some silly reasons. I noticed everything. It was right in front of me. How could I not?

When I returned and found him asleep on the couch, my heart melted at the sight. He looked so utterly endearing. He is silly, he is goofy, and sometimes he makes me question my own existence. But he is also beautiful, not just on the outside, but deep within.

He has a way of making me smile with his antics, always putting in the effort to make me happy. He hugs me when I need it the most, and he makes breakfast for me, even though he whines incessantly when I'm not around or I give attention to someone who is not him.

In his own unique, heartfelt way, he shows me just how much he cares.

He was asleep, and a voice inside me screamed, "Kiss him-- he'll never know."

Oh, how I liked the idea. Of course I did. Hadn't I wanted to do that for so long, no matter how much I told myself that I didn't? But I did. Maybe a bit too much.

I leaned over him, my heart racing frantically. The idea of my lips touching his filled me with a mix of excitement and nervousness. But before I could do anything, his eyes fluttered open, and I jerked back, standing up and fumbling with my words. "Uh, umm, I was waking you, but you pulled my hand in your sleep, so--" I stammered.

He didn't notice my awkwardness, groggy as he was, and I thanked all the heavens for it. My mind was crowded with all kinds of questions. What if he had realized what I was trying to do? What would he think?

When we finally lay on the bed that day, his hand accidentally brushed against mine under the covers. Neither of us moved. We left our hands where they were, connected by that simple, but electrifying touch.

Every morning, I always found myself in his embrace, waking up to the warmth and safety of his arms around me. But in that moment, the touch of our hands under the covers meant more than any embrace. It wasn't just him wanting to feel my touch, it was me, too, longing for his.

___

(Refrence - when they were going to crash Ellisa's wedding)

I helped him put on the jacket, and after doing so, I ran my hand over the lapels, a soft smile tugging at my lips. I took a few steps back to look at him properly and, with a smile playing on my lips, remarked casually, "Not bad."

"Just 'not bad'?" he countered. "You were practically speechless yesterday when you laid eyes on me in this." I agreed with him, but I wasn't going to admit it.

"Don't flatter yourself," I retorted. "I'm not going to say that you're looking utterly charming and have the capability to pull anyone you want right now."

He took a step towards me. "Hmm, did you realize you just praised me?"

Oh, I absolutely did praise him. "No, I didn't," I said, feigning innocence.

"You did," he said, a wide grin spreading across his face. He looked so happy, as if my praise had sent him on cloud nine.

Placing both my hands gently against his chest and applying slight pressure, I admitted, "Okay, okay, you look stunning right now. I might just find myself falling for you."

Raising his eyebrow, he asked teasingly, "You might?"

'Oh, May be I already am,' -- my inner voice spoke.

__

"Wait a minute," he said, approaching the cupboard and taking out a shopping bag, handing it to me. I was confused as to why he was giving me anything, but when I opened it, I realized it was the same shirt he had asked me to buy.

"This shirt-" I began, my gaze shifting between him and the clothing in my hands.

"Yes, I bought it for you," he confessed with a mischievous grin. Did my heart melt a little? No, it melted a lot because it's not about the shirt; it's about his efforts and these small gestures.

"You didn't have to," I protested for the sake of it.

"But I wanted to," he insisted. "Try it on. I have a feeling it will suit you perfectly."

When I emerged wearing the black satin shirt, the sight left him awestruck. I rolled one sleeve, and before I could move to do the other, he took the opportunity. With a gentle touch, he held the other sleeve and started rolling it up. I let him do it without any protest.

He fixed my gold chain. "My choice is really good," he said, not looking at the shirt but looking into my eyes. The intensity in his eyes made me blush, making me want to hide my face in my palms-or perhaps in his chest.

"I mean the shirt, it's my choice, right?" he said teasingly.

"Of course, you mean the shirt," I responded defensively.

___

“Oh really? Then prove it,” the bridesmaid said, her tone was challenging.

“How?”

“I don’t know, do something… kiss him.” As soon as the words left her mouth, my demeanor momentarily cracked. But hadn’t I wanted to do that for the longest time? Kiss him? Feel his lips against mine? And wasn’t this the perfect time, an excuse handed to me on a silver platter? I couldn’t let this chance slip away. Could I?

I barely registered Shubman saying, “Listen--” before he could complete the sentence I was on my feet.  I seized the lapel of his tuxedo and pulled him towards me, pressing my lips against his.

In that instant, all the uncertainty, confusion, and fear I had melted away. The feel of his lips on mine was surreal, more perfect than I had ever imagined. I wanted to move my lips, to deepen the kiss, but before I could, he moved his, and nothing could compare to that moment. People always talked about butterflies in their stomachs, but in that moment, I felt an entire zoo. How could a mere kiss feel so blissful?

I felt his hand snake around my waist, pulling me closer. I started to reciprocate, losing myself in the kiss. In that moment, there was nothing else-- It was just me, him, and the kiss.

___

(Reference - Shubman's confession)

"I want to say something, or more like confess something," he spoke hesitantly, his voice barely above a whisper. He waited for my reaction, but I kept pretending to be asleep.

When he was sure I was asleep, he began to pour out his heart. As he spoke, confessing his feelings for me, all I could think of 'didn't? didn't I feel the same?'

He continued, his voice was filled with vulnerability, "Sometimes, I’m afraid you can read my eyes and see what I feel for you,"  Yes, I could read it in his eyes. They were never subtle - the gleam in them, the stolen glances, the way they softened, especially when they gazed at mine.

All the while, he caressed my cheek with his thumb, his touch gentle and soothing. My chest rose and fell steadily, but inside, I struggled to maintain my calm facade.

His confession caught me off guard, and while he believed I was asleep, I absorbed every word, every emotion. He planted a soft kiss on my forehead, sending a shiver down my spine. "Ishan, I lo--" he hesitated, his voice trembling. "I really like you, Ishan," he finally said.

I should have kept my eyes shut, but the overwhelming need to see his face at that moment compelled me to open them slowly. When I did, I saw his eyes quickly close, but not before I caught the panic in them.

Finally, I knew his true feelings, and he had no idea that I shared them completely.

A few moments passed, and then I leaned in, planting a soft, gentle kiss on his forehead while caressing his cheek just as he had done while confessing. I hoped to relay the message that I completely reciprocated his feelings, but he was too dumb to understand anything.

______________________________________________________________________________________

"We've found a CCTV footage," the detective says, clicking on a video on her laptop. The screen shows a blurry image of Shubman getting into the back seat of a white car. "This was taken the day he went missing."

"So you have a lead. Find out about the car and its owner," Rohit bhaiya urges his voice tense.

"We found a much clearer footage and--" she clasps her hands together, taking a deep breath. "We identified the car."

Her words hangs in the air, and suddenly  the weight on my heart become unbearably heavy

"In the clear footage, Mr. Gill wasn't willing to get into the car. He was forced to... They were kidnapping him."

My heart sinks.

~~~

Agle chapter me pakka mil jayega sahi salamat 😔👍.

Title: Chapter : 33
Chapter_Text:
If anyone missed the last update (Chapter 32), then please read it. It is Ishan's POV of moments that happened between him and Shubman in the whole story!

••

Warning: please read this chapter while keeping you brain aside, because I wrote it keeping aside mine.

Because wtf I just wrote?(I'll pretend as if this wasn't pre planned-)

Anyway let's get into the chapter!

___________________________________________

•ISHAN KISHAN•

"Kidnapped?" everyone gasps collectively.

The detective nods grimly.

We exchange looks of utter disbelief, numerous questions swirling in our minds: who has kidnapped him, and why would anyone target him?

The other detective speaks up. "I know you all must be shocked to hear this, but it's true."

"But why would they kidnap him? What would anyone gain by kidnapping him?" Hardik Bhai asks, slowly recovering from the initial shock.

"The thing is, they didn't intend to kidnap Mr. Gill."

Our expressions shift from shock to confusion.

"Remember that man with the wrist tattoo, the one Mr. Bumrah mistook for Mr. Gill? That person is a rival of a notorious gang. When they came to kidnap him, they ended up kidnapping Mr. Gill by mistake because of their similar appearance."

"If they kidnapped him by mistake, then why are they still holding him hostage?" Shreyas asks the detectives.

"The leader of the gang is not in India at the moment," one of them replies. "They'll only take action once he returns." She pauses and picks up her iPad. "And this is good news for us because they won't harm or do anything to him, at least not until the leader comes back."

She holds up the iPad, showing us a picture. "This is the property where they are keeping him right now," she says. The image reveals a large white bungalow, standing isolated in an otherwise deserted area.

"We have a plan, and we are going to execute it this evening," the detective declares firmly.

My heart races frantically, drowning out half of the conversation. All I hear is the blood rushing in my ears. Someone's hand lands on my shoulder, and I don’t bother to look. The squeeze is meant to be reassuring, but it only fuels my mind with the worst possible scenarios. I try to focus on the discussion happening around me.

"We also want to come," one of us says.

"No, you can't," the detective refuses.

After a heated debate, they relent slightly. "Okay, I’ll allow four or five of you, but you must stay outside. Only us and the cops will go inside for the rescue. We understand your emotions and respect them, but you all have to obey us in this situation."

________

I finally take a proper look at the bungalow and realize it has two distinct sections: a front portion that indeed looks like a 'bungalow' and a rear portion that appears crumpled and weary, desperately in need of renovation.

My phone rings, and I excuse myself from the group, starting to walk parallel to the property. Once I'm far enough from their sight, My step turn toward the back part of the bungalow- The phone ring was fake.

To reach the rear section, I have to cross a small, neglected garden, its overgrown plants and bushes obscuring the view of the back of the building.

There are some guards stationed at the front, but no one is on the other side, which is a good sign.

The dry leaves crunch under my feet as I cross the garden, the bungalow growing more eerie with each step I take. A small door waits at the top of a few worn steps, its weathered surface hinting that it hasn't been used in years.

There are also two windows situated at both ends, but they can't be seen easily as they are hidden by overgrown bushes. When I try peeking through them, I meet with disappointment.

Frustration rises within me. Maybe I should go back to where everyone is and let the detectives do their work. But then, something catches my eye. Just below the left window, there are a few dense shrubs deliberately hiding a secret passage. I can barely make out a small square cut in the wall, cleverly concealed and stuffed with a wooden block.

Trying my luck, I chop off the shrub with the help of the knife. Yes, I have a knife with me because you never know! I am grateful for my foresight in bringing it.

The hidden passage comes into view, and I gently pull at the wooden block. To my surprise, it slides out easily, revealing the entrance.

When I step through the entrance, I realize there are steps ahead. I descend carefully, discovering a basement with no light source except for the light coming from the passage behind me. Each footfall echoes in the empty space, prompting me to quickly remove my shoes. I move slowly, barely able to make out anything, but manage to situate the faint outline of steps on the far side.

Reaching those stairs, I climb up and find another door. At first, I think it is locked, but soon realize it is jammed. After a struggle that feels like fifteen minutes, I manage to force it open. I take a cautious look around. There is no one in this part, or at least it seems so. Oddly, the interior looks far more ordinary than the eerie exterior.

I carefully close the door and start taking measured steps forward, hiding behind walls and pillars. I finally reach an area where I can see two rooms: one with a digital lock and one without.

I stand near the one with the digital lock, and something within me says that they have kept him here. I press my ear against the door but hear nothing.

As I stand here, contemplating my next move, a sudden commotion from the opposite side startles me. Someone is coming. My heartbeat quickens, and I swiftly run to hide behind a nearby pillar. The footsteps grow louder, and my mouth goes dry.

Peering cautiously from behind the pillar, I see a man approach the door and start punching numbers into the digital lock. I squint my eyes to get a better look-- one-nine-one-one.

He twists the knob, and the door opens. He enters quickly. I wait patiently, my nerves taut. It doesn’t take long before he reemerges, locks the door, and leaves. I take a deep breath and start moving toward the door.

I stand there, ready to punch in the code, when a loud noise startles me. Someone has definitely seen me. I am doomed.

Slowly, I turn my head to see if anyone is behind me. To my relief, there’s no one. But then I hear more noises, this time realizing they’re coming from another part of the bungalow. The cops might have entered.

Shifting my focus back to the digital lock, I start tapping the number : 1-9-1-1.

I twist the knob, its surface cool under my palm. My insides twist with anxiety as the knob moves.

The door is open.

Slowly, I push the door. My eyes take a moment to adjust to the darkness inside. The only light filters through a small lancet window.

The room is sparse. The only furniture I can make out is a small couch.

And then I see him. He's standing beside the couch, his face turned toward the window, one hand supporting himself on the armrest. Relief surges through me, a wave of emotion so powerful it nearly knocks me off balance. I can't describe in words how much this moment means. "Shubman," I call out, my voice barely a whisper. He turns his head in my direction.

He stands straight, and I see his features clearly now. His eyes look droopy, and his expression is pained. My chest tightens at the sight, and I quickly close the gap between us. Before he can comprehend my presence, I wrap my arms around him tightly. His body tenses at first, but then, realizing it's me, he relaxes and holds me just as tightly. He buries his face in the crook of my neck, and I begin to rub his back soothingly.

When I try to break the hug, he clutches the fabric of my T-shirt, making a fist unwilling to let me go.

After standing like that for I dont know how long, I slowly pull back, running my fingers along his face, searching for any cuts or injuries. Shubman takes my hands, and brings them to his lips and kisses them gently,  before hugging me again. We stay like that, holding each other, finding solace in the embrace until -

*Thump thump*

A loud noise from outside the room startles us, breaking our hug. Despite his dizziness, Shubman instinctively steps in front of me, his tall frame shielding me. Before I can fully process what's happening, the door flings open.

For a moment, horror grips me. Then, as the figures come into focus, I realize they're cops. My shoulders relax with the understanding.

Someone flicks on the lights, illuminating the room. I see the detectives entering with Rohit, Virat, and Hardik Bhaiya.

Their demeanor relaxes when they see an unharmed Shubman standing there, but their expressions turn abashed when their eyes land on me. I brace myself for their wrath.

Rohit Bhaiya is the first to step forward. He hugs Shubman and ruffles his hair, trying to lighten the mood. "You know how tense I was, thinking about who I'd pick as my opening partner," he says, and Shubman lets out a low chuckle.

Then Virat Bhaiya embraces him, his voice stern as he threatens Shubman never to end up in a situation like this again.

Hardik Bhaiya follows, kissing Shubman on the cheek and patting his back while giving him a hug

All of them, though, shoot daggers at me with their eyes. As we start to leave the room, Shubman suddenly passes out. Panic grips us all, but the detective quickly reassures us. "It's okay, no need to panic. This happens a lot in situations like these. They must have given him something. We're going to take him to the hospital to examine if there's any kind of drug in his system."

When we finally step out of the bungalow, a chill runs down my spine. We see a plethora of police officers and detectives making arrests, it’s terrifying, truly.

I get into the ambulance with Virat Bhaiya, while Rohit and Hardik Bhaiya follow us.

~~~

Okay! So we are going to forget that I ever wrote about Shubman's kidnapping and Ishan's foolishness. I mean, when the detectives were doing their work, what was the need to go inside?
